+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) ACQUIRES MAJORITY STAKE IN PROSOL GROUP TO GROW FRANCE FRESH FOOD NETWORK

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Acquires Majority Stake In Prosol Group To Grow France Fresh Food NetworkApollo closes the purchase of a majority stake in Prosol Group through its managed funds, with Ardian selling its stake. The deal was finalized on May 7, 2026. Prosol Group operates nearly 450 stores across France under Grand Frais and fresh brands and runs a vertically integrated supply chain centered on quality and freshness. Jean-Paul Mochet will remain Chief Executive Officer, leading the company as it integrates into Apollo’s portfolio.The investment aligns with Apollo’s strategy to broaden its footprint in consumer-focused platforms. Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) reports approximately $1.03 trillion in assets under management, underscoring its scale to support Prosol’s expansion plans. UBS AG provided financial advisory services, with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard also advising.The deal terms were not disclosed, and potential risks include supply chain volatility and changing consumer tastes. Apollo will monitor integration milestones and ensure alignment with its broader investment objectives while pursuing growth in France’s fresh food sector.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS RECORD AUM AT MORE THAN $1 TRILLION AS IT PILOTS DAILY PRIVATE ASSET PRICING

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) reported that total assets under management surpassed $1 trillion for the first time as it advances a daily pricing pilot for its private assets. On the 6th of May 2026, during the first-quarter results call, Marc Rowan, chairman and chief executive, described activity as reflecting solid demand and noted record fee-related earnings. Total AUM reached $1.03 trillion as of March 31, 2026, up 31 percent from a year earlier. The company posted first-quarter inflows of $115 billion and inflows of $300 billion in the trailing twelve months. The asset management business brought in $95 billion of inflows, supported by Athora's acquisition of the Pension Insurance Corporation in the UK, along with institutional and global wealth inflows into credit-focused strategies. Retirement services added $20 billion in inflows. The Financial Stability Board released a report highlighting vulnerabilities in private credit on the same day, and Apollo defended its approach, citing prudent underwriting. The group also described its ongoing daily valuation pilot, which produced estimated daily values for investment-grade fixed income products last year and will provide daily pricing for corporate investment-grade assets by 6:30 PM ET and for direct lending and asset-backed finance by 9:30 PM ET. Looking ahead, Apollo sees momentum into 2026 with targets for 20 percent growth in fee-related earnings and 10 percent growth in SRE, supported by a strong origination pipeline and disciplined capital management, as institutional demand for private credit remains elevated.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) PARTNERS WITH ANTHROPIC, BLACKSTONE AND GOLDMAN SACHS ON $1.5 BILLION AI VENTURE

On the 6th of May 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) launches a $1.5 billion enterprise AI services venture with Anthropic, Blackstone and Goldman Sachs to deliver AI tools for mid-market businesses and private equity portfolio companies. The venture aims to bolster operational efficiency for mid-sized clients that lack resources to build AI capabilities in-house.The collaboration signals a strategic shift toward collaboration over traditional balance sheet capital as Apollo seeks to capture productivity gains and build new fee-based revenue streams within its portfolio.In parallel, Apollo is raising capital for Hybrid Value Fund III and Accord Fund VII, which have closed at approximately $6.5 billion and $1.9 billion, respectively. The firm’s profitability data show a margin of 11% versus 17.9% a year earlier, and its dividend history remains unstable. The stock trades about 25.2% below one estimate of fair value, suggesting potential upside if the AI project and funds meet expectations.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) RAISES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS FUND; FIRST CLOSE EXPECTED IN SPRING

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, reflecting ongoing investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The fund’s first close is expected to occur in spring, per remarks from Chief Executive Michael Arougheti on an earnings callArougheti described the fund as having "very strong demand" after the solid performance of prior vintages. Ares Management completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalBeyond Japan, Ares has advanced other capital initiatives, including Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets that include a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, co-president of Ares, said the firm is fundraising to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, largely tied to commitments to its seventh special situations fund, SSG Fund VIIReturning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is reviewing a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account sharing a mandate similar to the SSG series, focused on non performing loans and single-asset distress in Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) EXPANDS RETAIL PLATFORM WITH US$1.7 BILLION WHITESTONE REIT PURCHASE

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has agreed to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding its footprint in necessity-based retail centers across major U.S. metro areas. + QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO REOPENS DOHA–BAGHDAD FREIGHT LINK WITH HIGHER CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, reestablishing the Doha to Baghdad cargo link. Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will launch a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad, paired with twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route from 10th May to provide additional belly-hold capacity.The combined cargo capacity is set to exceed 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad from 10 May, reflecting an integrated approach that blends dedicated freighter uplift with passenger capacity.The move comes as Qatar Airways Cargo continues scaling its network. The freighter network now reaches more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while the passenger network is expected to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June. Bookings are open via Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales channels. For shipments, contact cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or your local representative. The resumption sits within a broader network strategy, supported by a fleet of 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, and a road feeder network as the airline continues its investments in digitalization, products, and infrastructure.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES ERBIL ROUTE IN PHASE, EXPANDS IRAQI CONNECTIVITY

Qatar Airways will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the carrier 's return to direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a phased reinstatement of its Iraq operations. The airline will restart services to three Iraqi cities — Erbil, Baghdad (BGW) and Basra (BSR) — in a staged rollout aimed at rebuilding its regional network and reconnecting key Middle Eastern destinations.Freighter operations to Baghdad will resume on 7th May, reinforcing cargo and trade links with Iraq and supporting broader network restoration. These resumptions follow earlier reinstatements to Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus and Kozhikode as part of a wider strategy to restore its global network.By mid-June 2026, the carrier expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide, with Erbil travellers gaining access to the Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, which the airline describes as a pivotal connector linking six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESTARTS DUBAI, BAHRAIN AND KOZHIKODE SERVICES, TARGETS 150-DESTINATION EXPANSION

Qatar Airways is resuming a broad set of routes as part of a measured rebound in regional travel, with key services in the Middle East, Asia, and Europe back on the timetable. As of 5th May 2026, the carrier confirmed the reinstatement of flights to Dubai and Sharjah, marking a milestone in stabilizing operations in the region.In addition, daily flights to Bahrain (BAH) and Kozhikode (CCJ) will resume, beginning 1st May 2026. The airline outlined a gradual plan to rebuild its global network, aiming to operate more than 150 destinations starting 16th June 2026. The extended schedule will be in effect through 15th September 2026, reflecting responsiveness to evolving travel demand.Qatar Airways emphasized passenger preparation during this transition, urging travelers to check its website or mobile app for updates and verify contact details. Those with confirmed bookings will receive direct notices if any changes occur, reflecting the carrier's customer service commitment.The restored routes include connections to several in-demand destinations, catering to both business and leisure travelers. The broader recovery trend in aviation is underscored by the carrier's proactive network expansion, and analysts will be watching Qatar Airways' performance as it navigates a changing travel landscape.

urn:contentItem:6JJ9-5613-S8F8-00VK-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:53ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREAN ASSET MANAGER'S NEW SPACE TECH ETF REACHES 100 BILLION WON AUM, HIGHLIGHTING NEWSPACE TILT

The fund has posted a 7.63% return, the best among Korea’s nine space and aerospace ETFs. Individual investors have shown robust demand, net purchasing 52.6 billion won over the six-session window after launch. Portfolio manager Kim Hyuntae emphasized agility in active management, noting that the team can quickly adapt to market developments and incorporate new listings like SpaceX as opportunities arise.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION UNVEILS 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) said it will host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, launching on 20th April 2026, to deepen the capabilities of finance professionals across public and private sectors.Over four days the program will deliver eight lectures, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for 2026.Participants will engage in discussions of alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, and will review post-investment portfolio management from both investment-management and operational perspectives.The curriculum will address key market issues, such as the evolving U.S. interest-rate environment and the implications of the institutionalization of stablecoins. Interest in the academy is high, with applications from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, KIC's CEO, stressed that the organization will continue to develop domestic finance by sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network, and that the academy will nurture global investment professionals through initiatives like this program. Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals.

+ KIC SPOTLIGHTS NORTH AMERICAN MID-MARKET INFRASTRUCTURE AT NYC FORUM

On April 16, 2026, the Korea Investment Corporation hosted the 28th New York Finance Forum at the Korea Society in New York, drawing more than 40 participants from government, public investment bodies and financial services to discuss mid-market infrastructure opportunities in North America.Northleaf Capital Partners shared insights on the mid-market landscape, stating that deals typically involve companies valued around USD 1 billion or less, with the threshold now expanding to as much as USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics and asset-manager criteria.Jared Waldron, Co-Head of Infrastructure at Northleaf, noted that roughly 86% of North American infrastructure transactions occur in the mid-market, but institutional funds remain heavily concentrated in larger funds, resulting in a less competitive environment and potentially better entry points for medium-sized deals.Jessica Kennedy, Managing Director at Northleaf, stressed that mid-market infrastructure can deliver differentiated results through active value creation and multiple exit strategies, implying attractive risk-adjusted returns for participants. Yuryoung Kim, Head of KIC's New York office and organizer of the forum, said the discussions could help domestic institutional investors refine overseas alternative investment strategies, as KIC maintains a presence in major hubs including New York, San Francisco, London, Singapore and Mumbai to facilitate information exchange and collaboration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CIC SELLS $1 BILLION PORTFOLIO TO GOLDMAN AND ARDIAN AT MIXED DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs Asset Management and Ardian have agreed to acquire a $1 billion CIC portfolio at discounts, marking a significant entry in the secondary market. Ardian secured a double-digit markdown on its portion, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, according to people familiar with the matter, with the deal dated 15th April 2026.The seller, China Investment Corporation, is moving to rebalance its holdings and reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. As of 2024, about 48.5% of CIC's assets were in alternatives, and around 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally. CIC had previously explored offloading stakes in funds managed by Carlyle, KKR and TPG, paused the process, and then reopened discussions that led to the sale to Goldman and Ardian.For Goldman and Ardian, the acquisition expands their scale in the highly active secondary market. Ardian has recently raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion. The deal also mirrors a broader trend among large investors—Harvard and several Asian asset managers among them—turning to secondaries to obtain liquidity and rebalance portfolios amid concerns about prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk.

+ BLUEFIVE TO LEAD GP ROLE IN CICC-HBIS FUND OF FUNDS TARGETING CROSS-BORDER INDUSTRIAL UPGRADES

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The appointment was formalized at a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds aims to raise RMB32 billion over 15 years and will invest through a mix of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS's industrial priorities, spanning traditional sectors and emerging areas such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.The vehicle will focus on cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading, with its governance built around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—intended to connect local governments, international capital, and industrial developers. BlueFive will partner with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to help ensure long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, noted the firm’s optimism about China’s long-term growth potential and highlighted opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion, especially in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group is among the world’s largest steel materials producers and has been a Fortune Global 500 member for 17 consecutive years. CICC, based in Beijing, offers a full suite of financial services including investment banking, asset management, and private equity, and the partnership with BlueFive aims to boost regional investment opportunities and industrial growth.

+ CANADA SEES $60 BILLION IN FDI FROM CHINA AND HONG KONG; US REMAINS MAJOR SOURCE

Canada's foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong exceeded $60 billion through 2023, making these regions the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta accounted for roughly half of the total, with Statistics Canada recording $24.9 billion from China and $32.8 billion from Hong Kong, behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.As of December 30, 2025, The Globe and Mail reported that Alberta’s engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors remained strong despite strained federal relations with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse said federal-provincial discussions had intensified, describing the moment as 'harvest time' for those who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta notes that the investments focus on Alberta’s traditional oil and gas sector, carbon capture projects, green-energy initiatives and tourism. China remains a major customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline, highlighting ongoing economic interdependence even as Ottawa and Beijing sought to reset ties after past tensions from the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians between 2018 and 2021. Diplomatic engagement has continued, including a July 2024 visit by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly to Beijing, and a visible thaw under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met Xi Jinping in October 2025 and is being considered for a 2026 visit. Christiaanse added that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials for engaging with China, arguing that disengaging from China 'simply does not exist.' Canada’s broader FDI profile shows a heavy reliance on the United States (about 45.7% in 2023) with Asia-Oceania accounting for 11.8% (led by Japan, Hong Kong and China).

urn:contentItem:6JJ7-P0T3-S31K-S1X1-00000-00May 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: SWINERTON INCORPORATED & PEER INSIGHTS2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:53ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: SWINERTON INCORPORATED & PEER INSIGHTS

Swinerton Incorporated remained active across public infrastructure, mission critical construction, and regional leadership development during 2026. On April 16, 2026, Swinerton Builders was selected by San Francisco Public Works as the construction manager/general contractor for a new $145 million San Francisco Fire Department training facility. The project, located in the city's southeastern district, is designed to modernize emergency response training with a 50,000-square-foot scenario district replicating San Francisco neighborhood conditions, including sloped intersections, narrow hallways, and post-earthquake structural simulations. The campus is also set to include emergency medical services training, onsite fire apparatus instruction, and Neighborhood Emergency Response Team sessions. The administration and classroom building is planned as an Essential Services Building for immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and as a backup emergency operations center. Paul Hinz, vice president and division manager for Swinerton's San Francisco/East Bay division, said the center would equip first responders with critical training resources, while project executive Christina Pantera said the company would apply experience from more than 250 San Francisco projects, including over 40 public safety facilities, to deliver the site in coordination with city stakeholders and community members.

Earlier in the month, on April 8, 2026, Swinerton announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical, a move tied to the company's expansion in data center and mission critical infrastructure. Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of experience in complex project delivery and has previously launched and scaled a national mission critical and data center construction division, as well as managed strategy and procurement for a major technology company's Americas data center program. In the role, she is expected to lead Swinerton's mission critical strategy with an emphasis on speed-to-market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Chris Chany, chief revenue officer, said her appointment would strengthen growth in the sector and improve client experience. The company also pointed to her expertise in areas such as cooling technologies and renewable energy integration as relevant to evolving client demand.

That expansion theme continued on March 16, 2026, when Swinerton Builders announced leadership changes in its Northeast Division. Jay Quackenbush was promoted to vice president and division manager, with Nicholas Schifferle joining as director of client relations and Robert Murphy taking on the role of project executive. Quackenbush brings nearly 30 years of construction experience and longstanding familiarity with New York City building conditions. Ray Haj, chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, said the promotion would improve the division's ability to meet a broad range of client needs, from facility management to large ground-up assignments. Schifferle added more than 20 years of international business development experience spanning Asia, Australia, and the United States, while Murphy arrived with oversight experience on more than $650 million in commercial interiors work. Since entering the Northeast market in 2021, the division has completed office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up projects across all five boroughs and has continued expanding into education, residential, and public work.

Across the peer group, Balfour Beatty reported a combination of operational expansion and financial momentum. On April 1, 2026, the company promoted Trent Johnson to vice president of operations in the Carolinas, with responsibility for leading and growing its aviation sector in North and South Carolina. Johnson has more than 19 years of construction experience, including over 12 years with Balfour Beatty, during which he contributed to 22 aviation projects ranging from targeted renovations to multi-phase programs. His work has included projects at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. Jeff Sandeen, president of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, said Johnson had built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner in local aviation operations and credited his ability to guide teams through active airport environments as a key driver of regional success.

Financially, Balfour Beatty highlighted growth supported by a record order book. On March 11, 2026, the company said market valuation had risen by nearly two-thirds over the previous year, with underlying earnings per share up 9% to 47.6 pence. Operating profit in support services rose by one-third to £122 million, while underlying margin in that segment improved by 80 basis points to 8.5%. The company said growth was supported by power transmission demand in the UK and higher building volumes in the US, although cost overruns in one US civils project remained a factor. It also said its UK construction division achieved margin targets ahead of schedule, reflecting improved operational performance.

Hensel Phelps was linked to one of the more visible construction technology announcements in the group through Track3D's expansion across its portfolio. On April 1, 2026, Track3D said Hensel Phelps had entered a multi-year agreement to deploy its Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects, extending a relationship that began with a single pilot. The rollout is intended to standardize progress monitoring across the contractor's portfolio and improve early issue detection to reduce delays and improve schedule reliability. A case study at San Francisco International Airport was cited as evidence of the platform's ability to shift project teams from reactive problem-solving to proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and use of existing field staff. Track3D also used the update to highlight new enterprise features, including an admin dashboard, groups functionality, and capture-free progress tracking on floorplans, all aimed at large contractor oversight and workflow efficiency.

Hensel Phelps also made a leadership move in Hawaii. On April 1, 2026, Micah "Pono" Choy was promoted to project superintendent on the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. Choy joined the company in 2018 and has contributed to aviation, wastewater, and federal projects in Hawaii and Guam. The company said the promotion reflected both project experience and a collaborative leadership style that has helped build strong partnerships across teams.

Quanta Services used acquisition activity and investor messaging to underline its positioning in utility, renewable, and energy infrastructure. On April 6, 2026, the company completed its acquisition of Davenport-based Tri-City Group, a contractor specializing in electrical construction and industrial services. Tri-City said it would continue operating under its existing name and brand, and Lori Rochholz, director of marketing, said customers would continue to see the same leadership and responsiveness, now with access to Quanta's broader resources. Quanta also referenced additional acquisitions including Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service. President and chief executive officer Duke Austin said the acquisitions expanded the company's craft-skilled platform to support critical electric utility programs across the United States. Tri-City, which traces its roots to 1895 and has worked across 46 states and multiple countries, remains active through subsidiaries including Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy.

Before that, on March 3, 2026, Quanta Services used the Jefferies Power, Utilities, & Clean Energy Conference to outline its growth outlook for 2026. Management pointed to clean energy demand, infrastructure development, data center market opportunities, and relationships with midcap utilities such as NIPSCO as part of its strategic direction. The company said it remained focused on organic growth, disciplined capital allocation, and strategic acquisitions, while presenting a framework centered on long-term value creation and risk management.

Barton Malow's recent updates reflected continued involvement in sports and civic infrastructure. On April 28, 2026, the company was named construction manager for Detroit City FC's new 15,000-seat stadium, AlumniFi Field, on the site of the former Southwest Detroit Hospital. The stadium is scheduled to open in spring 2027 and is intended to address demand beyond the club's current 7,000-seat Keyworth Stadium, where attendance has regularly exceeded capacity. The venue is planned between Corktown and Mexicantown and will include a natural grass pitch, three permanent grandstands with canopies, and space for community events in addition to soccer matches. Senior vice president Sean Hollister said the project aligned with Barton Malow's commitment to technical excellence and community engagement, while Detroit City FC chief executive officer Sean Mann said the stadium would reflect the character and authenticity of Detroit.

Barton Malow also joined March-Westin as construction manager for the Milan Puskar Stadium West Tower Project at West Virginia University, with work set to begin after the 2026 football season. The joint venture is responsible for preconstruction planning, procurement, schedule and cost management, and onsite coordination. Regional executive Scott Mull said the company would bring the same focus and craftsmanship it has applied to athletic facilities nationwide, while March-Westin president Jamie Ridgeway said the combination of local knowledge and national stadium experience would support delivery with minimal disruption to fans.

DPR Construction's updates centered on talent, office growth, and workplace recognition. On April 29, 2026, the company said it would expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet with local support from Henrico County. The additional space is intended to support workforce growth, improve collaboration, and strengthen operational capacity in the region. DPR said the expanded office would incorporate modern design features and sustainable practices, with completion expected within the following year.

A month earlier, on April 1, 2026, DPR appointed Jason Page to lead its Nashville Special Services Group. Page has more than 14 years of experience and has been with DPR since 2012. Since relocating to Nashville in 2021, he has led corporate interior and entertainment assignments under tight deadlines. The company said he would oversee a group responsible for a broad mix of work, from targeted improvements to major interior buildouts, while also continuing to mentor project teams and share best practices for high-impact, quick-turn delivery.

On March 31, 2026, DPR also announced Great Place To Work Certification, with 97% of employees saying it is a great place to work, a score the company said was 40 points above the average US workplace benchmark. Chief executive officer George Pfeffer said the certification was especially meaningful because it came directly from employees and reflected a culture of trust, support, and growth. DPR linked the recognition to broader learning and development programs and to a decentralized operating model that encourages initiative and open information sharing across its workforce of more than 11,000 professionals.

Turner Construction's recent news flow was led by large-scale data center, healthcare, and mixed-use assignments. On April 28, 2026, the company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, completing the structural phase of a 72-megawatt facility less than five months after groundbreaking. The 450,000-square-foot building sits on a 75-acre campus and includes a two-story structural steel design, insulated precast exterior system, and hybrid cooling technology combining liquid and air-cooled systems. Turner said more than 80,000 craft hours had been logged to date, with around 345 skilled workers contributing. Operations manager Nick Sevier said the milestone reflected the strength of Turner's partnership with Aligned, the dedication of trade partners, and the company's commitment to delivering a complex, high-performance facility safely and efficiently.

Also on April 28, 2026, Turner said vertical construction had begun on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Pennsylvania. The 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower is expected to expand bed count from 356 to 500, increase operating rooms from 12 to 18, enlarge the emergency department, and support the health system's private-room strategy. Turner said the project is expected to generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact, support up to 3,800 construction jobs, and expand onsite labor from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak periods. Vice president and senior general manager David Kaminski said the project would expand access to modern healthcare while supporting caregivers and the wider community.

PCL Construction combined culture, infrastructure, and digital capacity themes in its announcements. On April 2, 2026, the company said it had been named to the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For list for the 17th time, ranking No. 91. Deron Brown, president and chief operating officer of US operations, said the employee-owned structure helped shape a culture in which people feel trusted, supported, and invested in the future. The company linked the recognition to its standing as No. 11 on Engineering News-Record's Top 400 Contractors list for 2025 and to its track record delivering complex projects across North America.

PCL also highlighted progress on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center near Denver. On March 19, 2026, PCL Construction Services and PCL Industrial Services announced structural completion of the AI-ready facility, which spans approximately 277,000 square feet across five steel-frame colocation halls on a 17-acre site. The center is designed for enterprise-scale artificial intelligence and graphics processing workloads and includes a separate administration building. PCL Industrial Services is self-performing core electrical work, including power systems integration, while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems. Vice president and district manager Ryan Schmidt said the company's mission critical and data center experience remained central to executing high-density projects of this type. Construction is scheduled for completion in summer 2027.

JE Dunn Construction focused its recent updates on manufacturing strategy, workforce development, and sector leadership. On April 6, 2026, the company launched Form Off-Site Solutions, a new initiative specializing in design for manufacturing for complex construction components. Led by off-site manufacturing director Nick Effenheim, the operation offers metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies. JE Dunn said the model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, project safety, quality, and cost certainty while helping address labor shortages and schedule pressure. Effenheim said the ability to move ideas and concepts into a manufactured environment was a differentiator, while president and chief executive officer Gordon Lansford described the business as a step forward in the company's effort to drive innovation for clients.

On March 19, 2026, JE Dunn also celebrated the topping out of the Northland Workforce Development Center in Kansas City, Missouri. The 145,000-square-foot, two-story project is intended to expand access to career and technical education in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. The milestone included a beam-signing ceremony involving the Platte County R-3 School District and other stakeholders. Project executive Brad Hokanson said the project was about creating pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals, while Brian Noller of the Platte County School District said the initiative represented a long-term regional investment supported by state, municipal, business, and community partners. Substantial completion is targeted for 2027.

McCarthy Holdings paired healthcare execution with sustainability reporting in its recent releases. On February 6, 2026, the company reached topping out on the $3.7 billion California Tower at UC Davis Health, part of an eight-year Vision 2030 expansion plan on the Sacramento campus. The 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion are expected to add approximately 334 inpatient beds along with operating rooms, imaging, pharmacy, and burn care facilities. UC Davis Health said the opening is expected in 2030. McCarthy noted that the tower is the final component of a plan intended to expand the campus from 3.6 million square feet to more than 7 million square feet.

McCarthy then issued its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas Emissions Report on February 16, 2026. Developed with Gravity, the report covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and selected Scope 3 categories, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 results independently audited and awarded limited assurance. Senior director of sustainability Ian Pope said the work provided teams with practical data to meet regulatory requirements and aligned with the company's employee-owned structure and core values. McCarthy also highlighted biodiversity initiatives, fuel management efforts, use of digital tools and artificial intelligence in decision-making, and a photovoltaic recycling program using Solar Energy Industries Association-approved recyclers.

Clark Construction Group's recent news included a senior HR appointment. On April 29, 2026, Carly Reiner Caceres joined as director of human resources for national campus recruiting. The company said her background across people operations, talent strategy, employer branding, and full-cycle recruiting would support early-career talent pipelines nationally. Before joining Clark, she held leadership roles at Proper, MILLER, Accenture, and Grant Thornton. The appointment comes as Clark continues to invest in long-term talent development across its building and infrastructure operations.

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART EXPANDS FORTRESS PARTNERSHIP WITH $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW AGREEMENT

Upstart Holdings announced a forward-flow commitment from Fortress affiliates to buy up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated on its platform over 15 months, with the disclosure on 5th May 2026.The deal expands a partnership that began in 2025 and reflects Upstart’s effort to broaden its network of capital partners. Fortress and its affiliates join more than 100 banks and credit unions using Upstart’s AI-driven models and cloud applications to originate loans.Upstart’s platform covers a broad suite of products, including personal loans, automotive finance and refinance loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product.Executives described the arrangement as a win for both sides, noting that a larger base of capital partners helps reduce borrowing costs and complexity for borrowers, while Fortress seeks differentiated, high-quality consumer credit opportunities for its investors. The collaboration signals a broader industry shift toward technology-driven credit solutions and positions both companies to pursue opportunities in the evolving lending landscape.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH USD 180 MILLION PREFERRED-NOTE FINANCING

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is designed to support the construction of the land-based facility and to help meet growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores Fortress Investment Group’s longstanding presence in Japan and its willingness to provide both capital and strategic guidance to Pure Salmon Japan. The closing signals strong institutional backing for the project and aligns with Fortress’s broader focus on sustainable infrastructure.The project targets a production capacity of 10,000 tonnes and aims to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will use advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to minimize imports and bolster food security.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress officials also noted the value of the partnership; Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, called the investment a meaningful step in strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure. As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan intends to pursue additional long-term equity partners to enhance operations.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST DEAL, AGEWELL TO OPERATE AS PART OF GROWTH IN SENIOR LIVING PORTFOLIO

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida. The transaction, funded by Fortress affiliates, was completed on 12th March 2026, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care.Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, serving an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast.Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, said: \"We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families.\"The acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE: KKR) PARTNERS WITH XPV WATER PARTNERS TO TRANSFER AXIUS WATER TO CRH

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and XPV Water Partners announce the divestment of Axius Water to CRH, the global building materials group. The deal was confirmed on 6th May 2026 at 18:26:48. The transaction aims to maximize value for stakeholders as CRH expands its water management capabilitiesThe sale is expected to strengthen CRH's ability to offer innovative water solutions, a growing area in today’s environmental landscape. KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and XPV Water Partners, known for their water investments, have positioned Axius Water for a smooth transition under CRH's stewardshipAxius Water's technologies and solutions are recognized for advancing performance in the water sector, aligning with CRH's sustainability objectives. The deal underscores KKR's strategy to focus on high-potential sectors while XPV Water Partners realizes value from their investment. Financial details of the transaction have not been disclosedThe arrangement reflects a broader trend in private equity toward streamlining operations and focusing on core competencies, enabling CRH to leverage Axius Water's strengths in the market

+ VERTIS INFRASTRUCTURE TRUST (NYSE:VIT) TARGETS UP TO ₹3,000 CRORE VIA IPO AND SECONDARY SALE

Vertis Infrastructure Trust (NYSE:VIT) plans to file a draft red herring prospectus by end May 2026 to raise up to ₹3,000 crore through a combination of primary and secondary share sales. The InvIT portfolio centers on toll and annuity road projects across India, built largely through acquisitions by KKR's Asian infrastructure fund which launched the platform in 2021 and expanded in 2024-25 with the acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The trust oversees 28 road projects across nine states, valued at ₹26,500 crore.Insiders note that the final offer size could be trimmed as the trust has limited requirements for new primary capital. The pricing and scale will reflect this cautious assessment within a competitive InvIT landscape. Investors have shown continued interest in InvITs as a way to access long-duration, infrastructure-backed yields.Recent sector activity underscores the momentum in InvIT fundraising, with NHAI raising over ₹9,000 crore through two InvIT offerings in March and Citius Transnet Investment Trust raising ₹1,105 crore in April, reinforcing demand despite market volatility. Vertis Infrastructure Trust remains focused on executing a disciplined and scalable IPO strategy

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) SETS $10 BILLION BUDGET FOR AI INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) will invest $10 billion toward AI-focused power plants and data centers, a move that expands capital dedicated to AI infrastructure in the United States.On 3rd May 2026, the commitment sits within a broader industry effort, with Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta collectively directing more than $100 billion worldwide into similar projects.The investment is intended to secure long-term power agreements, giving KKR leverage in power generation as demand from AI data centers climbs.The plan also intersects geopolitics, as Canada and Germany consolidate activity to counterbalance U.S.–China AI influence; KKR's participation could influence this evolving landscape.Market signals connect the investment to Google's AI development trajectory, and analysts weigh the potential for Google's stock to rise toward $310 as the infrastructure builds out.

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) EXPANDS HEALTHCARE REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT CAPABILITIES WITH KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM DEAL

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, expanding its healthcare portfolio. The terms of the deal are not disclosed in a release dated May 4, 2026. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the new RCM platform, aligning with Carlyle's plan to fuse Knack's analytics with Rao's global operations to bolster advisory servicesKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM count physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals among their clients. The collaboration targets rural cost reports and complex anesthesia cases, as noted by Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle frames the move as a platform for broader participation in the RCM market and signals intent to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementCarlyle sets its course to strengthen capabilities in healthcare management and revenue cycle technology, with the expectation of delivering innovative solutions that support providers in navigating a changing healthcare landscape

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) UNITES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO EXPAND DIGITAL HEALTHCARE SERVICES PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is integrating Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a unified platform designed to boost digital capabilities across healthcare services. The move addresses ongoing structural changes in the U.S. healthcare system, including administrative complexities, workforce challenges, and evolving care delivery models. The combined platform will support physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers requiring tailored operational assistance, leveraging Knack's globally distributed operational framework and Equalize's tools for predictive analytics and optimization. The integration aims to advance revenue cycle management by enhancing billing, claims processing, and payer coordination, potentially improving efficiency and regulatory compliance. The initiative aligns with market trends as providers seek technology-driven solutions to navigate operational complexities, and Carlyle positions itself to participate in a sector undergoing substantial transformation.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) NAMES THREE CO-PRESIDENTS TO SHARPEN EXECUTION AND STRATEGY

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective 22nd April 2026, to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as the firm refines its strategy in a slower macro environment.The appointments signal a structural shift intended to sharpen execution across investment, fundraising, and operational priorities at Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) as rates rise and deal activity slows.Schwartz has emphasized clearer accountability and faster decisions since taking the helm, and the co-presidents model mirrors arrangements seen at other large private equity firms to share oversight of key levers.Investors will watch how the changes influence Carlyle Group's investment activity and quarterly results in the quarters ahead.

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL PROVIDES $300 MILLION FINANCING TO ESTABLISHMENT LABS

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan from Oaktree Capital Management. The facility, finalized on 3rd May 2026, refinances existing debt through 2031 and reserves US$35 million for growth initiatives.The loan carries a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure with flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets, enhancing funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues its operational and expansion plans.The investment thesis centers on the Motiva implant platform and minimally invasive procedures expected to drive broad adoption.Management guidance for 2026 projects revenue of US$264-266 million, implying roughly 25-26% growth from the prior year. The financing should ease refinancing pressure and provide liquidity to address losses and cash burn without an immediate equity raise.While the capital raise improves flexibility, there are risks linked to ongoing cash burn and negative free cash flow. Some analysts call for around 30% revenue growth with earnings near US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could reinforce or temper optimism about profitability. The agreement marks a strategic shift in its financing approach.

+ BIOXCEL AND OAKTREE EXTEND REGISTRATION RIGHTS, ISSUE PRIVATE WARRANTS

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed in an 8-K filing that it has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and a group of institutional holders. The move aligns with the company's ongoing efforts to optimize its capital structure and provides a framework for potential future equity transactions under the arrangement disclosed on 18 April 2026. Key elements of the deal include amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was signed on 27 March 2026, setting the stage for these warrants. On 15 April 2026, the company issued the unregistered warrants in a private placement, a mechanism that avoided immediate Securities Act registration as part of the financing strategy. BioXcel said it would file registration statements with the SEC to enable possible resale of the warrants' underlying shares, a move designed to improve liquidity and maintain investor confidence. Lenders have indicated they intend to hold the securities for investment purposes rather than seek rapid resale, which could moderate near-term volatility in the stock price. Market participants should note the potential dilution risk if warrants are exercised, and the registration process could increase the total share count. The financing approach signals the company's liquidity needs and strategic objectives, rather than an implicit endorsement of aggressive share issuance. BioXcel also clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting and regulatory requirements.

+ OAKTREE PARTNERS WITH NORTH MILL ON $355.8 MILLION EQUIPMENT FINANCE SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management, in partnership with North Mill Equipment Finance, has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization called OAKEF 2026-1. The closing occurred on 8th April 2026 and signals strong investor appetite for quality equipment finance assets.The collateral consists of equipment finance assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal completed in November 2025.The offering was rated from AAA to single A and attracted peak subscriptions of approximately 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack. The deal delivered a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%.Truist Securities served as the lead structuring agent, with Deutsche Bank acting as co-manager.Brendan Beer, portfolio manager for Oaktree's asset-backed finance and structured credit platform, said the market response reflected investor confidence in Oaktree's equipment strategy and highlighted the North Mill collaboration as a key driver.David Lee, chairman and CEO of North Mill Equipment Finance, stressed that the partnership demonstrates asset quality and the servicing platform's strength, noting that NMEF will continue as primary servicer for the assets.The securitization reinforces Oaktree's position in asset-backed finance and shows its ability to attract significant investor interest and structure deals that meet partner financing objectives.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC SECURES $1.25 BILLION INVESTMENT FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT (NYSE:APO) THROUGH CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED SHARES

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC has secured $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., through the issuance of convertible preferred shares that grant Apollo a 13% ownership stake. The transaction was formalized on April 20, 2026 and remains subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The deal values the company at a post-money of approximately $9.62 billion, with the new capital intended to enhance operational capabilities and broaden market reach.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) POSTS 13% MINORITY STAKE IN MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS VIA $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The move aligns with McKesson's strategy to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and provides capital to advance strategic initiatives. The convertible preferred structure allows Apollo to participate in potential upside while McKesson maintains a staged path toward the separation. The investment underscores a strategic alignment between the two firms as McKesson seeks to optimize its core businesses. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, signaling investor confidence in growth opportunities. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells and one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will monitor the implications for McKesson's separation plans and for Apollo's portfolio strategy.

+ APOLLO CLOSES GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP DEAL TO BOLSTER UK BUILD-TO-RENT PLATFORM

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK housing footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a transaction completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed.The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans.GLG is recognized as a leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired more than 5,000 homes.Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios and helping GLG access capital from both Apollo and third-party partners to accelerate development.Gatehouse Bank said the sale will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) REPORTS $53.0 BILLION OF U.S. DIRECT LENDING CLOSED ACROSS 348 TRANSACTIONS OVER THE 12 MONTHS THROUGH MARCH 31, 2026

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) RAISES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS FUND; FIRST CLOSE EXPECTED IN SPRING

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, reflecting ongoing investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The fund’s first close is expected to occur in spring, per remarks from Chief Executive Michael Arougheti on an earnings callArougheti described the fund as having "very strong demand" after the solid performance of prior vintages. The fundraising comes more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares Management completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalBeyond Japan, Ares has advanced other capital initiatives, including Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets that include a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, co-president of Ares, said the firm is fundraising to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, largely tied to commitments to its seventh special situations fund, SSG Fund VIIReturning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is reviewing a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account sharing a mandate similar to the SSG series, focused on non performing loans and single-asset distress in Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) EXPANDS RETAIL PLATFORM WITH US$1.7 BILLION WHITESTONE REIT PURCHASE

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has agreed to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding its footprint in necessity-based retail centers across major U.S. metro areas. The deal signals progress in Ares' strategy to deepen exposure to essential consumer needs within real estate and related assets. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, and appointed Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong to lead the Asia Special Situations team, reflecting the firm's push to grow credit and special situations in Asia. Whitestone's inclusion strengthens Ares' position in retail real estate and aligns with a broader plan to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types, including private credit and real assets. Investors will assess how Whitestone integrates with Ares' existing operations and the progress of Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape. The deal introduces potential risks such as profit margin compression and higher leverage, while the dividend yield of 5.38% is not fully supported by earnings

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO REOPENS DOHA–BAGHDAD FREIGHT LINK WITH HIGHER CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, reestablishing the Doha to Baghdad cargo link. Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will launch a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad, paired with twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route from 10th May to provide additional belly-hold capacity.The combined cargo capacity is set to exceed 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad from 10 May, reflecting an integrated approach that blends dedicated freighter uplift with passenger capacity.The move comes as Qatar Airways Cargo continues scaling its network. The freighter network now reaches more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while the passenger network is expected to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June. Bookings are open via Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales channels. For shipments, contact cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or your local representative. The resumption sits within a broader network strategy, supported by a fleet of 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, and a road feeder network as the airline continues its investments in digitalization, products, and infrastructure.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES ERBIL ROUTE IN PHASE, EXPANDS IRAQI CONNECTIVITY

Qatar Airways will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the carrier 's return to direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a phased reinstatement of its Iraq operations. The airline will restart services to three Iraqi cities — Erbil, Baghdad (BGW) and Basra (BSR) — in a staged rollout aimed at rebuilding its regional network and reconnecting key Middle Eastern destinations.Freighter operations to Baghdad will resume on 7th May, reinforcing cargo and trade links with Iraq and supporting broader network restoration. These resumptions follow earlier reinstatements to Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus and Kozhikode as part of a wider strategy to restore its global network.By mid-June 2026, the carrier expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide, with Erbil travellers gaining access to the Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, which the airline describes as a pivotal connector linking six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESTARTS DUBAI, BAHRAIN AND KOZHIKODE SERVICES, TARGETS 150-DESTINATION EXPANSION

Qatar Airways is resuming a broad set of routes as part of a measured rebound in regional travel, with key services in the Middle East, Asia, and Europe back on the timetable. As of 5th May 2026, the carrier confirmed the reinstatement of flights to Dubai and Sharjah, marking a milestone in stabilizing operations in the region.In addition, daily flights to Bahrain (BAH) and Kozhikode (CCJ) will resume, beginning 1st May 2026. The airline outlined a gradual plan to rebuild its global network, aiming to operate more than 150 destinations starting 16th June 2026. The extended schedule will be in effect through 15th September 2026, reflecting responsiveness to evolving travel demand.Qatar Airways emphasized passenger preparation during this transition, urging travelers to check its website or mobile app for updates and verify contact details. Those with confirmed bookings will receive direct notices if any changes occur, reflecting the carrier's customer service commitment.The restored routes include connections to several in-demand destinations, catering to both business and leisure travelers. The broader recovery trend in aviation is underscored by the carrier's proactive network expansion, and analysts will be watching Qatar Airways' performance as it navigates a changing travel landscape.

value.Title: May 07, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO PEER GROUP UPDATE: KEY RECENT NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHS-S813-S8K8-R2R8-00000-00May 07, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO PEER GROUP UPDATE: KEY RECENT NEWS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:19ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 07, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO PEER GROUP UPDATE: KEY RECENT NEWS

On February 25, 2026, Consigli Construction Co. commenced work on the Williams College Museum of Art, marking the institution's first purpose-built facility on campus. Abigail Berkebile, a figure at Consigli Construction, highlighted the complexity and unique design of the project, which aims to enhance student access to art collections. Berkebile also utilized the announcement to emphasize the diverse opportunities available for women in the construction industry, noting that project teams rely on broad expertise spanning accounting, legal matters, IT, and procurement, allowing professionals to advance without a traditional field background.

In peer news, Suffolk Construction continues to manage the Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project, which facilitated the reopening of a long-term westbound lane closure on U.S. Route 58 in Virginia on May 5, 2026. The project is designed to build a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to improve safety and regional efficiency. Separately, on May 1, 2026, Virgin Media O2 announced a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement to source renewable energy from a 70-megawatt solar farm currently under construction in Suffolk. The solar farm is anticipated to commence power generation in 2027 and will supply approximately 5 percent of the telecommunications company's total energy demands as it targets Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040.

Turner Construction Company achieved a significant milestone on April 28, 2026, with the topping out of the 450,000-square-foot, 72-megawatt Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland. Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company, stated, "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame. It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner's commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." On the same day, Turner Construction announced the start of vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Pennsylvania. The project features an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will increase capacity to 500 beds by the first quarter of 2028. Additionally, on April 22, 2026, Turner was named the general contractor for the $500 million Westcourt Orlando mixed-use development, which will feature a hotel, residential units, and an entertainment venue.

Gilbane Development announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets on April 28, 2026. Operating out of the New York City office, Vlahakis is tasked with refining the firm's funding strategy and leading transaction structuring. In other company developments, Gilbane launched leasing for Phase II of its $135 million affordable housing redevelopment at 7th Street and Barnaby Road in Washington, D.C., on April 3, 2026. Slated for completion by summer 2026, the second phase comprises 229 units across four buildings, with twenty percent of the apartments designated as Permanent Supportive Housing managed by the district's Department of Homeless Services.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHN-7S43-SK5D-3246-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:53ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREAN ASSET MANAGER'S NEW SPACE TECH ETF REACHES 100 BILLION WON AUM, HIGHLIGHTING NEWSPACE TILT

The fund has posted a 7.63% return, the best among Korea’s nine space and aerospace ETFs. Individual investors have shown robust demand, net purchasing 52.6 billion won over the six-session window after launch. Portfolio manager Kim Hyuntae emphasized agility in active management, noting that the team can quickly adapt to market developments and incorporate new listings like SpaceX as opportunities arise.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION UNVEILS 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) said it will host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, launching on 20th April 2026, to deepen the capabilities of finance professionals across public and private sectors.Over four days the program will deliver eight lectures, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for 2026.Participants will engage in discussions of alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, and will review post-investment portfolio management from both investment-management and operational perspectives.The curriculum will address key market issues, such as the evolving U.S. interest-rate environment and the implications of the institutionalization of stablecoins. Interest in the academy is high, with applications from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, KIC's CEO, stressed that the organization will continue to develop domestic finance by sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network, and that the academy will nurture global investment professionals through initiatives like this program. Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals.

+ KIC NAMES JAEHYUN JOO AS CRO TO BOLSTER RISK MANAGEMENT AND INVESTMENT OVERSIGHT

Korea Investment Corporation has appointed Jaehyun Joo as Chief Risk Officer and Executive Vice President of the Investment Management Division. The move was disclosed on April 17, 2026, after a thorough review by the Executive Committee, and Joo will begin a three-year term immediately.Joo’s career spans more than two decades in financial risk and policy. He started at Korea Ratings KR in 1999 before moving to the Bank of Korea in December 2005, where he held multiple leadership roles in the investment domain.During his time at these institutions, he led the Investment Strategy Team, the Investment Planning Team, and most recently the Reserve Management Group, gaining broad exposure to portfolio construction and risk analytics.At KIC, Joo will aim to leverage his international finance experience to strengthen the corporation’s investment-management framework, reinforcing a foundation for long-term, stable asset management amid shifting global markets. His appointment is expected to bolster KIC’s risk management capabilities and support the firm’s broader strategic objectives.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CIC SELLS $1 BILLION PORTFOLIO TO GOLDMAN AND ARDIAN AT MIXED DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs Asset Management and Ardian have agreed to acquire a $1 billion CIC portfolio at discounts, marking a significant entry in the secondary market. Ardian secured a double-digit markdown on its portion, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, according to people familiar with the matter, with the deal dated 15th April 2026.The seller, China Investment Corporation, is moving to rebalance its holdings and reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. As of 2024, about 48.5% of CIC's assets were in alternatives, and around 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally. CIC had previously explored offloading stakes in funds managed by Carlyle, KKR and TPG, paused the process, and then reopened discussions that led to the sale to Goldman and Ardian.For Goldman and Ardian, the acquisition expands their scale in the highly active secondary market. Ardian has recently raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion. The deal also mirrors a broader trend among large investors—Harvard and several Asian asset managers among them—turning to secondaries to obtain liquidity and rebalance portfolios amid concerns about prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk.

+ BLUEFIVE TO LEAD GP ROLE IN CICC-HBIS FUND OF FUNDS TARGETING CROSS-BORDER INDUSTRIAL UPGRADES

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The appointment was formalized at a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds aims to raise RMB32 billion over 15 years and will invest through a mix of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS's industrial priorities, spanning traditional sectors and emerging areas such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.The vehicle will focus on cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading, with its governance built around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—intended to connect local governments, international capital, and industrial developers. BlueFive will partner with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to help ensure long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, noted the firm’s optimism about China’s long-term growth potential and highlighted opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion, especially in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group is among the world’s largest steel materials producers and has been a Fortune Global 500 member for 17 consecutive years. CICC, based in Beijing, offers a full suite of financial services including investment banking, asset management, and private equity, and the partnership with BlueFive aims to boost regional investment opportunities and industrial growth.

+ CANADA SEES $60 BILLION IN FDI FROM CHINA AND HONG KONG; US REMAINS MAJOR SOURCE

Canada's foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong exceeded $60 billion through 2023, making these regions the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta accounted for roughly half of the total, with Statistics Canada recording $24.9 billion from China and $32.8 billion from Hong Kong, behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.As of December 30, 2025, The Globe and Mail reported that Alberta’s engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors remained strong despite strained federal relations with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse said federal-provincial discussions had intensified, describing the moment as 'harvest time' for those who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta notes that the investments focus on Alberta’s traditional oil and gas sector, carbon capture projects, green-energy initiatives and tourism. China remains a major customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline, highlighting ongoing economic interdependence even as Ottawa and Beijing sought to reset ties after past tensions from the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians between 2018 and 2021. Diplomatic engagement has continued, including a July 2024 visit by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly to Beijing, and a visible thaw under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met Xi Jinping in October 2025 and is being considered for a 2026 visit. Christiaanse added that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials for engaging with China, arguing that disengaging from China 'simply does not exist.' Canada’s broader FDI profile shows a heavy reliance on the United States (about 45.7% in 2023) with Asia-Oceania accounting for 11.8% (led by Japan, Hong Kong and China).
