Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHB-0NR3-RVT7-63BW-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:20ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FOUNDATION SIGNALS STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN RESKILLING AS AI ADVANCES

The Rockefeller Foundation on 28th April 2026 unveiled a grant-based nationwide effort to address AI-related disruption in the job market, aiming to prevent displacement and guide workers toward roles that complement automation. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development and places emphasis on program design, metrics and evaluation, with plans to measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to gauge the impact of automation. Funding will be provided through grants, pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The organization notes that implementation choices will determine whether the initiative yields scalable reskilling pipelines or a patchwork of pilot projects. Viewed as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative, the foundation hopes to catalyze new models of retraining and public-private collaboration, while contributing to policy discussions on workforce development such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It anticipates a rising demand for data-driven evaluations in the skills and labor analytics ecosystem as the program unfolds.

+ CROSS-SECTOR COLLABORATION AND FUNDING DRIVE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION'S BALTIMORE ECONOMIC AGENDA

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, drawing more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business to Baltimore to discuss strategies for strengthening the economy and expanding opportunity.A centerpiece announcement was a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, with a target of about 1.6 million new jobs across sectors such as healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence.The Foundation also committed $12 million to Invest in Our Future to promote clean energy opportunities and growth in underserved areas, while Maryland Governor Wes Moore announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards for education and childcare in high-poverty areas. The event also highlighted the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and emphasized partnerships with the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next gathering is planned for Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ WINTER PROPOSES GLOBAL DATA COLLABORATION TO STRENGTHEN ONE HEALTH FOR CHILDREN

On April 21st, 2026, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, called for data unification at the One Health Summit. He told attendees that despite vast data resources, information remains siloed across sectors, hindering decisions and allocation of resources, and he reiterated the need for cross sector collaboration with a quote: "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." Winter said the call for data integration aligns with earlier remarks by Oracle co founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025, who urged a unified platform to consolidate health data across nations amid about 3,000 databases. For illustration, he cited the drought in Somalia: the Rockefeller Foundation is funding a local tech incubator developing a dashboard that links data from health, nutrition, climate and agriculture to inform local actors and international donors, including the diaspora contributing roughly $2 billion annually.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ NHRC APPLAUDS FORD FOUNDATION'S LONGSTANDING PARTNERSHIP IN NIGERIAN HUMAN RIGHTS WORK

On Apr. 28, 2026, the NHRC in Abuja recognized the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its long-running support to human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony brought together Commission executives and was attended by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu lauded the Foundation for backing efforts to expand access to justice and to strengthen the Commission's governance and operations. He noted that tackling Nigeria's human rights challenges requires sustained cooperation with development partners to translate policy into practical outcomes for citizens.Aniagolu-Okoye said the recognition underscores the Foundation's ongoing commitment to both awareness and practical justice. She emphasized that rights education must be paired with strong enforcement mechanisms, and she highlighted the Foundation's role in supporting citizens to seek remedy when rights are violated.The event underscored the value of continuing public-private collaboration, with Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, acknowledging the MOVE project and the dashboard system as key areas advanced with Foundation support.

+ SOFT POWER OF ECOWAS FIRST LADIES HERALDED AS KEY TO GBV REFORMS, FORD FOUNDATION WARNS

The Ford Foundation urged First Ladies at the ECOWAS Forum on 24th April 2026 to harness their influence to combat gender-based violence, arguing that the soft power of First Ladies can drive social change and strengthen accountability.It highlighted the continued prevalence of GBV, noting that one in three women globally are affected and that child marriage remains a concern in West and Central Africa, with many girls married before 18.Economic costs were cited as a compelling reason for action, with GBV-related losses reaching up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, surpassing investments in some priority sectors such as education.While First Ladies are not formal policymakers, the Ford Foundation stressed their essential role in shaping public discourse and decision-making, pointing to Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as leaders whose campaigns elevated women’s issues and prompted national responses. Such examples demonstrate how strategic use of soft power can catalyze systemic change.The Foundation reiterated ongoing investments in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, including partnerships with First Ladies and governors’ spouses to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based services, and challenge harmful norms. It emphasized that laws alone are insufficient without public trust and accessible support for survivors.

+ FORD FOUNDATION BETS $60 MILLION ON CIVIC PARTICIPATION AND ELECTION INTEGRITY

The Ford Foundation unveiled a $60 million initiative to bolster democracy in the United States, announced on April 14, 2026. The grants will fund nonpartisan groups focused on expanding civic participation and protecting voting rights, across the ideological spectrum, including Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. The program builds on the foundation's Civic Engagement and Government effort, which has awarded more than $1 billion in grants over its history. President Heather Gerken described the move as a proactive step to safeguard the integrity of elections and public trust, adding, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment." Campaign Legal Center founder Trevor Potter emphasized voting access as essential, and Pillars of the Community co-chairs Ben Ginsberg and Bob Bauer highlighted bipartisan support for electoral integrity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: April 29, 2026: GIP and Grupo M xico Forge Power Generation Alliance with Saavi Energ a Assets

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-GWJ3-RVKP-T3GY-00000-00April 29, 2026: GIP and Grupo M xico Forge Power Generation Alliance with Saavi Energ a Assets2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:53ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 29, 2026: GIP and Grupo M xico Forge Power Generation Alliance with Saavi Energ a Assets

Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP) has entered into a definitive agreement to combine its power generation assets with those of Grupo México, specifically integrating those owned by Saavi Energía S.à.r.l. The agreement comes into effect following the announcement made on 29th April 2026, marking a significant milestone in the energy sector.

This strategic combination will see Grupo México holding a 70% stake in the newly formed entity, while GIP will retain a 30% ownership. The merger aims to create a diversified power generation platform that will encompass 10 conventional and renewable power plants located in high-demand areas across the country, ensuring a robust operational synergy and enhanced scale.

The combined output of the new entity is anticipated to reach 4,510 MW, bolstered by a project pipeline of approximately 5,000 MW. The collaboration is designed to leverage the strengths of both GIP and Grupo México, positioning the merged entity as a formidable player in the energy market, particularly in the wake of rising demand for sustainable energy solutions.

Latham & Watkins LLP played a crucial role in advising GIP during this transaction, building on its previous advisory work for GIP in the original acquisition of Saavi Energía. The corporate team, led by partners Antonio Del Pino and Carlos Ardila, provided comprehensive legal support, including guidance on tax, antitrust, energy regulation, and environmental matters.

With a focus on creating operational efficiencies and ensuring compliance with regulatory frameworks, the team from Latham & Watkins included various specialists across multiple legal domains. This multifaceted approach underscores the complexity of the transaction and the strategic importance of GIP's collaboration with Grupo México in the evolving landscape of power generation.

Global Infrastructure Partners operates as an investment company. The Company invests in the natural resources infrastructure, water distribution and treatment, power and utilities, air transport infrastructure, seaports, and waste management sectors. Global Infrastructure Partners serves customers worldwide.

The Foundation emphasizes interventions for workers rather than product development, centering on program design, metrics and evaluation to track displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions caused by automation. Funding will flow through grants, programmatic pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments; decisions during implementation will matter for whether the effort yields scalable reskilling pipelines or fragmented pilots without lasting impact. As a strategic philanthropic signal, the move is not regulatory or corporate and aims to catalyze innovative retraining models and strengthened public-private collaboration, aligning with broader policy debates on universal basic income, sectoral training and apprenticeships. Stakeholders expect a growing emphasis on measurable, data-driven evaluations within skills and labor analytics, with close attention to funding priorities and partnership dynamics, including durable credentialing and employer engagement, as well as placement metrics.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES MULTI-YEAR INITIATIVE TO CREATE 1.6 MILLION JOBS ACROSS DISTRESSED COMMUNITIES

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, drawing more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business in Baltimore to discuss strategies to revitalize the economy and expand opportunity across communities.In a centerpiece announcement, the Foundation unveiled a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, targeting roughly 1.6 million additional jobs across growth sectors such as healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, the Foundation's President, underscored the promise of restoring a stable life through hard work and resilience for American workers.The Foundation also allocated $12 million to Invest in Our Future to mobilize clean energy opportunities and drive growth in underserved areas. Maryland Governor Wes Moore participated and announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards to advance education and childcare in neighborhoods with high childhood poverty. The gathering also introduced the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and showcased cross-sector partnerships with the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next Big Bets for America gathering is scheduled to take place in Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ DATA SILOS HAMPER AID, ROCKEFELLER OFFICIAL SAYS, URGING CROSS-SECTOR INTEGRATION

On April 21st, 2026, at the One Health Summit, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, addressed a panel titled "Philanthropies for One Health, One Health for Children" and pressed for a unified data platform. He argued that, despite unprecedented data, information remains trapped in silos across sectors, hindering decision-making and resource allocation, and he warned, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." Winter noted that the broader push for data integration mirrors earlier calls by Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025, who urged consolidating health data across nations into a single platform amidst about 3,000 fragmented databases. To demonstrate potential benefits, he pointed to Somalia’s drought crisis, where Rockefeller support is funding a local tech incubator to build a dashboard that unites health, nutrition, climate and food data to guide local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes around $2 billion annually. He closed by saying empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, merged with local information, could yield more effective and cost-efficient humanitarian responses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION AWARDED BY NHRC FOR COMMITMENT TO NIGERIA'S HUMAN RIGHTS AGENDA

On Apr. 28, 2026, in Abuja, the National Human Rights Commission honored the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its longstanding support of human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony drew senior NHRC officials and directors, and the honor was accepted by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.NHRC Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu praised the Foundation's steady backing, especially for initiatives that broaden access to justice and strengthen the Commission's capacity to protect rights. He stressed that Nigeria's human rights landscape is broad and complex, and that durable progress depends on ongoing partnerships with development partners rather than isolated action.Aniagolu-Okoye described the recognition as meaningful and encouraging, and reiterated the Foundation's commitment to equipping citizens with knowledge of their rights and support to pursue justice when those rights are violated. She warned that awareness alone is not enough without effective enforcement mechanisms and highlighted the Foundation's role in fostering stronger enforcement through collaboration and capacity building.The event also underscored the value of continued cooperation as the country confronts evolving human rights challenges. Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, expressed gratitude for the Ford Foundation's enduring partnership, citing the MOVE project and the Commission's dashboard system as notable advances that benefited from the Foundation's early support.

+ WEST AFRICA FIRST LADIES CALLED TO LEAD ON GENDER-BASED VIOLENCE, FORD FOUNDATION SAYS

At the ECOWAS First Ladies Forum on Zero Tolerance for GBV in Banjul on 24th April 2026, the Ford Foundation urged First Ladies across West Africa and the region to leverage their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence, framing the First Ladies' soft power as a catalyst for social change and stronger accountability.Aniagolu noted that despite existing frameworks and commitments, GBV remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with more than 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18.The Foundation highlighted the economic cost of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, a drain that rivals investments in key sectors such as education.The Ford Foundation stressed that First Ladies are not formal policymakers but can shape public discourse and influence decision-making across the continent. It cited Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as examples, noting that instruments like the Better Life for Rural Women Programme and Hands Off Our Girls campaign elevated women’s issues and contributed to national actions, underscoring the potential of soft power to drive systemic change.The Foundation has continued to invest in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, partnering with First Ladies and governors’ spouses in Nigeria to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based response systems, and address harmful social norms. Aniagolu argued that laws and policies alone are not enough without public trust and accessible services, pointing to persistent silence among survivors fueled by stigma, fear, and weak institutional confidence.

+ FORD FOUNDATION ALLOCATES $60 MILLION TO PROTECT DEMOCRACY AND RULE OF LAW

The Ford Foundation on April 14, 2026 announced a new $60 million grants program aimed at strengthening democratic processes nationwide. The grants will go to nonpartisan, nonprofit organizations dedicated to protecting the rule of law and voting rights. Recipients span the ideological spectrum, including Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. This initiative builds on a decade-long Civic Engagement and Government program, which has delivered more than $1 billion in grants over its history. President Heather Gerken described the effort as a proactive step to bolster democracy, stating, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment." Campaign Legal Center founder Trevor Potter emphasized the importance of voting access, saying, "Ensuring every American has access to voting is paramount for the health of the democracy." Pillars of the Community is co-chaired by Ben Ginsberg and Bob Bauer, representing a bipartisan push to uphold electoral integrity. The Ford Foundation carries a $16 billion endowment and has operated for 90 years.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREAN ASSET MANAGER'S NEW SPACE TECH ETF REACHES 100 BILLION WON AUM, HIGHLIGHTING NEWSPACE TILT

The fund has posted a 7.63% return, the best among Korea’s nine space and aerospace ETFs. Individual investors have shown robust demand, net purchasing 52.6 billion won over the six-session window after launch. Portfolio manager Kim Hyuntae emphasized agility in active management, noting that the team can quickly adapt to market developments and incorporate new listings like SpaceX as opportunities arise.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION UNVEILS 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) said it will host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, launching on 20th April 2026, to deepen the capabilities of finance professionals across public and private sectors.Over four days the program will deliver eight lectures, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for 2026.Participants will engage in discussions of alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, and will review post-investment portfolio management from both investment-management and operational perspectives.The curriculum will address key market issues, such as the evolving U.S. interest-rate environment and the implications of the institutionalization of stablecoins. Interest in the academy is high, with applications from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, KIC's CEO, stressed that the organization will continue to develop domestic finance by sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network, and that the academy will nurture global investment professionals through initiatives like this program. Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals.

+ KIC NAMES JAEHYUN JOO AS CRO TO BOLSTER RISK MANAGEMENT AND INVESTMENT OVERSIGHT

Korea Investment Corporation has appointed Jaehyun Joo as Chief Risk Officer and Executive Vice President of the Investment Management Division. The move was disclosed on April 17, 2026, after a thorough review by the Executive Committee, and Joo will begin a three-year term immediately.Joo’s career spans more than two decades in financial risk and policy. He started at Korea Ratings KR in 1999 before moving to the Bank of Korea in December 2005, where he held multiple leadership roles in the investment domain.During his time at these institutions, he led the Investment Strategy Team, the Investment Planning Team, and most recently the Reserve Management Group, gaining broad exposure to portfolio construction and risk analytics.At KIC, Joo will aim to leverage his international finance experience to strengthen the corporation’s investment-management framework, reinforcing a foundation for long-term, stable asset management amid shifting global markets. His appointment is expected to bolster KIC’s risk management capabilities and support the firm’s broader strategic objectives.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CIC SELLS $1 BILLION PORTFOLIO TO GOLDMAN AND ARDIAN AT MIXED DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs Asset Management and Ardian have agreed to acquire a $1 billion CIC portfolio at discounts, marking a significant entry in the secondary market. Ardian secured a double-digit markdown on its portion, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, according to people familiar with the matter, with the deal dated 15th April 2026.The seller, China Investment Corporation, is moving to rebalance its holdings and reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. As of 2024, about 48.5% of CIC's assets were in alternatives, and around 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally. CIC had previously explored offloading stakes in funds managed by Carlyle, KKR and TPG, paused the process, and then reopened discussions that led to the sale to Goldman and Ardian.For Goldman and Ardian, the acquisition expands their scale in the highly active secondary market. Ardian has recently raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion. The deal also mirrors a broader trend among large investors—Harvard and several Asian asset managers among them—turning to secondaries to obtain liquidity and rebalance portfolios amid concerns about prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk.

+ BLUEFIVE TO LEAD GP ROLE IN CICC-HBIS FUND OF FUNDS TARGETING CROSS-BORDER INDUSTRIAL UPGRADES

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The appointment was formalized at a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds aims to raise RMB32 billion over 15 years and will invest through a mix of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS's industrial priorities, spanning traditional sectors and emerging areas such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.The vehicle will focus on cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading, with its governance built around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—intended to connect local governments, international capital, and industrial developers. BlueFive will partner with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to help ensure long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, noted the firm’s optimism about China’s long-term growth potential and highlighted opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion, especially in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group is among the world’s largest steel materials producers and has been a Fortune Global 500 member for 17 consecutive years. CICC, based in Beijing, offers a full suite of financial services including investment banking, asset management, and private equity, and the partnership with BlueFive aims to boost regional investment opportunities and industrial growth.

+ CANADA SEES $60 BILLION IN FDI FROM CHINA AND HONG KONG; US REMAINS MAJOR SOURCE

Canada's foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong exceeded $60 billion through 2023, making these regions the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta accounted for roughly half of the total, with Statistics Canada recording $24.9 billion from China and $32.8 billion from Hong Kong, behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.As of December 30, 2025, The Globe and Mail reported that Alberta’s engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors remained strong despite strained federal relations with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse said federal-provincial discussions had intensified, describing the moment as 'harvest time' for those who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta notes that the investments focus on Alberta’s traditional oil and gas sector, carbon capture projects, green-energy initiatives and tourism. China remains a major customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline, highlighting ongoing economic interdependence even as Ottawa and Beijing sought to reset ties after past tensions from the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians between 2018 and 2021. Diplomatic engagement has continued, including a July 2024 visit by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly to Beijing, and a visible thaw under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met Xi Jinping in October 2025 and is being considered for a 2026 visit. Christiaanse added that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials for engaging with China, arguing that disengaging from China 'simply does not exist.' Canada’s broader FDI profile shows a heavy reliance on the United States (about 45.7% in 2023) with Asia-Oceania accounting for 11.8% (led by Japan, Hong Kong and China).

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: WEEKLY DEVELOPMENTS REPORT: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH7-9DR3-S2YK-H099-00000-00WEEKLY DEVELOPMENTS REPORT: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:53ZLexisNexisWEEKLY DEVELOPMENTS REPORT: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ MILBANK LLP [9 Press Releases]

+ MILBANK EARNS BROAD RECOGNITION ACROSS KEY PRACTICE AREAS IN VAULT 2027

Milbank LLP is ranked No. 9 for overall prestige in the 2027 Vault Law Rankings, derived from a survey of more than 20,000 associates. The firm leads in Project Finance and sits in the top 10 in Clean Tech/Renewable Energy No. 2, Banking & Financial Services No. 5, Bankruptcy/Restructuring No. 6 and Energy, Oil & Gas No. 6. Other top-10 placements include General Corporate Practice No. 9, Securities/Capital Markets No. 9, Mergers & Acquisitions No. 10 and Private Equity No. 10, with additional recognition in Securities Litigation No. 19, General Commercial Litigation No. 21, Business Outlook No. 30 and Formal Training No. 29.

+ COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK OF MILBANK LLP TO JOIN STANFORD LAW SCHOOL'S 2026 SUPREME COURT AT MIDTERM CONFERENCE

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak will participate in the Stanford Law School's annual Supreme Court at Midterm conference, set for May 1-2, 2026. The event gathers top Supreme Court practitioners to evaluate the judicial landscape and discuss how recent Supreme Court activity reflects broader legal trends. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Supreme Court and has worked on major Milbank matters, including VOS Selections v. Trump and Wolford v. Lopez. Her participation underscores Milbank's ongoing involvement in high-profile Supreme Court advocacy.

+ MILBANK'S LAUREN DOYLE JOINS PANEL ON CHAPTER 11 PLAN CONFIRMATION AT ABI NEW YORK CITY EVENT

Milbank partner Lauren Doyle will participate as a panelist at the American Bankruptcy Institute's New York City Bankruptcy Conference on Thursday, May 21, 2026, at the Marriott Marquis in New York. She will speak on the panel titled \"Confirmation Issues/Release\", examining developments shaping chapter 11 plan confirmation, including evolving standards for consensual releases, gatekeeper provisions, and lessons from the In re ConvergeOne Holdings Corp. decision. The discussion will also address how market uncertainty is affecting business projections and analyze plan implications arising from prepetition liability management exercises (LMEs). The ABI New York City Bankruptcy Conference is a premier gathering of insolvency and restructuring professionals, bringing together leading practitioners, judges, financial advisors and academics to discuss the most significant developments shaping the restructuring landscape.

+ COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK: SCOTUS IMPACT ON ERISA AND HEALTHCARE AT DRI CONFERENCE

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak spoke at the Defense Research Institute's (DRI) 2026 Life, Health, Disability and ERISA Seminar, held April 29 - May 1 in Atlanta. Her briefing focused on recent Supreme Court decisions relevant to ERISA and healthcare law. As a seasoned Supreme Court advocate, Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Court and briefed many more, and the event highlighted the importance of SCOTUS rulings for health and disability coverage. The DRI conference is a key forum for professionals in the health, insurance and ERISA sectors.

+ MILBANK ANTITRUST PARTNERS PUBLISH CPI PIECE ON DOJ SIGNALING GREATER SCRUTINY OF ENERGY AND DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE MARKETS

Milbank LLP antitrust partners Di Vincenzo and Schaeffer, with Ceccio, authored a CPI article. It notes DOJ signalsof rising interest in electricity generation and data-center deals overall.

+ ECOPETROL SECURES $1.25 BILLION SENIOR UNSECURED TERM LOAN; MILBANK REPRESENTS LENDERS

Milbank LLP advised the lenders on Ecopetrol S.A.'s $1.25 billion senior unsecured term loan facility. The financing will refinance existing indebtedness. It includes the credit facility used for Ecopetrol's acquisition of Interconexion Electrica S.A. E.S.P.

+ MILBANK CELEBRATES 53 LAWYERS ON ENGLAND & WALES 2026 PRO BONO RECOGNITION

Milbank reports that 53 of its lawyers are named on the 2026 Pro Bono Recognition List for England & Wales. The list honors barristers and solicitors who donated 25 hours of pro bono work in the prior calendar year. This acknowledgment underscores Milbank's continued dedication to public service and access to justice.

+ STM SECURES $630 MILLION GREEN RULE 144A PROJECT BONDS; MILBANK ACTS AS COUNSEL

Milbank LLP advised STM in connection with a $630 million issuance of 6.385% green Rule 144A senior secured project bonds due 2055. STM is a leading Chilean power transmission company and a subsidiary of Grupo Saesa, a joint venture between Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan ("OTTP") and Alberta Investment Management Corporation ("AIMCo"). The bond proceeds, together with funds raised in a Chilean market issue, were used to repay a $1.1 billion syndicated credit facility that financed Grupo Saesa's acquisition of Enel Transmision Chile, the principal power transmission company in Chile's metropolitan region.

+ MILBANK'S TRUSTS, ESTATES AND EXEMPT ORGANIZATIONS GROUP SECURES BAND 1 STATUS IN 2026 LEGAL 500 US PRIVATE CLIENT GUIDE

Milbank LLP's Trusts, Estates and Exempt Organizations Group has been ranked Band 1 for Private Wealth in New York in the 2026 Legal 500 US Private Client Guide. The guide also highlights leadership within the team, noting Austin Bramwell as a "Leading Partner" and Jessica Soojian as a "Recommended." The acknowledgement reinforces Milbank's standing in the private wealth arena.

value.Title: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING FORD FOUNDATION AND PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH1-B483-RVD8-229M-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING FORD FOUNDATION AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:53ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING FORD FOUNDATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION LAUNCHES GRANTS-DRIVEN INITIATIVE TO MITIGATE AUTOMATION'S JOB DISPLACEMENT

On 28th April 2026, the Rockefeller Foundation outlined a bold push to counter AI-driven workforce disruption with a grant-based national initiative designed to prevent displacement and shift workers into AI-complementary roles. The Foundation emphasizes interventions for workers rather than product development, centering on program design, metrics and evaluation to track displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions caused by automation. Funding will flow through grants, programmatic pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments; decisions during implementation will matter for whether the effort yields scalable reskilling pipelines or fragmented pilots without lasting impact. As a strategic philanthropic signal, the move is not regulatory or corporate and aims to catalyze innovative retraining models and strengthened public-private collaboration, aligning with broader policy debates on universal basic income, sectoral training and apprenticeships. Stakeholders expect a growing emphasis on measurable, data-driven evaluations within skills and labor analytics, with close attention to funding priorities and partnership dynamics, including durable credentialing and employer engagement, as well as placement metrics.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES MULTI-YEAR INITIATIVE TO CREATE 1.6 MILLION JOBS ACROSS DISTRESSED COMMUNITIES

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, drawing more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business in Baltimore to discuss strategies to revitalize the economy and expand opportunity across communities.In a centerpiece announcement, the Foundation unveiled a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, targeting roughly 1.6 million additional jobs across growth sectors such as healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, the Foundation's President, underscored the promise of restoring a stable life through hard work and resilience for American workers.The Foundation also allocated $12 million to Invest in Our Future to mobilize clean energy opportunities and drive growth in underserved areas. Maryland Governor Wes Moore participated and announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards to advance education and childcare in neighborhoods with high childhood poverty. The gathering also introduced the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and showcased cross-sector partnerships with the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next Big Bets for America gathering is scheduled to take place in Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ DATA SILOS HAMPER AID, ROCKEFELLER OFFICIAL SAYS, URGING CROSS-SECTOR INTEGRATION

On April 21st, 2026, at the One Health Summit, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, addressed a panel titled "Philanthropies for One Health, One Health for Children" and pressed for a unified data platform. He argued that, despite unprecedented data, information remains trapped in silos across sectors, hindering decision-making and resource allocation, and he warned, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." Winter noted that the broader push for data integration mirrors earlier calls by Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025, who urged consolidating health data across nations into a single platform amidst about 3,000 fragmented databases. To demonstrate potential benefits, he pointed to Somalia’s drought crisis, where Rockefeller support is funding a local tech incubator to build a dashboard that unites health, nutrition, climate and food data to guide local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes around $2 billion annually. He closed by saying empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, merged with local information, could yield more effective and cost-efficient humanitarian responses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION AWARDED BY NHRC FOR COMMITMENT TO NIGERIA'S HUMAN RIGHTS AGENDA

On Apr. 28, 2026, in Abuja, the National Human Rights Commission honored the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its longstanding support of human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony drew senior NHRC officials and directors, and the honor was accepted by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.NHRC Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu praised the Foundation's steady backing, especially for initiatives that broaden access to justice and strengthen the Commission's capacity to protect rights. He stressed that Nigeria's human rights landscape is broad and complex, and that durable progress depends on ongoing partnerships with development partners rather than isolated action.Aniagolu-Okoye described the recognition as meaningful and encouraging, and reiterated the Foundation's commitment to equipping citizens with knowledge of their rights and support to pursue justice when those rights are violated. She warned that awareness alone is not enough without effective enforcement mechanisms and highlighted the Foundation's role in fostering stronger enforcement through collaboration and capacity building.The event also underscored the value of continued cooperation as the country confronts evolving human rights challenges. Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, expressed gratitude for the Ford Foundation's enduring partnership, citing the MOVE project and the Commission's dashboard system as notable advances that benefited from the Foundation's early support.

+ WEST AFRICA FIRST LADIES CALLED TO LEAD ON GENDER-BASED VIOLENCE, FORD FOUNDATION SAYS

At the ECOWAS First Ladies Forum on Zero Tolerance for GBV in Banjul on 24th April 2026, the Ford Foundation urged First Ladies across West Africa and the region to leverage their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence, framing the First Ladies' soft power as a catalyst for social change and stronger accountability.Aniagolu noted that despite existing frameworks and commitments, GBV remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with more than 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18.The Foundation highlighted the economic cost of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, a drain that rivals investments in key sectors such as education.The Ford Foundation stressed that First Ladies are not formal policymakers but can shape public discourse and influence decision-making across the continent. It cited Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as examples, noting that instruments like the Better Life for Rural Women Programme and Hands Off Our Girls campaign elevated women’s issues and contributed to national actions, underscoring the potential of soft power to drive systemic change.The Foundation has continued to invest in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, partnering with First Ladies and governors’ spouses in Nigeria to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based response systems, and address harmful social norms. Aniagolu argued that laws and policies alone are not enough without public trust and accessible services, pointing to persistent silence among survivors fueled by stigma, fear, and weak institutional confidence.

+ FORD FOUNDATION ALLOCATES $60 MILLION TO PROTECT DEMOCRACY AND RULE OF LAW

The Ford Foundation on April 14, 2026 announced a new $60 million grants program aimed at strengthening democratic processes nationwide. The grants will go to nonpartisan, nonprofit organizations dedicated to protecting the rule of law and voting rights. Recipients span the ideological spectrum, including Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. This initiative builds on a decade-long Civic Engagement and Government program, which has delivered more than $1 billion in grants over its history. President Heather Gerken described the effort as a proactive step to bolster democracy, stating, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment." Campaign Legal Center founder Trevor Potter emphasized the importance of voting access, saying, "Ensuring every American has access to voting is paramount for the health of the democracy." Pillars of the Community is co-chaired by Ben Ginsberg and Bob Bauer, representing a bipartisan push to uphold electoral integrity. The Ford Foundation carries a $16 billion endowment and has operated for 90 years.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JH1-B483-RVD8-21FW-00000-00WEEKLY OVERVIEW: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:53ZLexisNexisWEEKLY OVERVIEW: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ MILBANK LLP [9 Press Releases]

+ MILBANK EARNS BROAD RECOGNITION ACROSS KEY PRACTICE AREAS IN VAULT 2027

Milbank LLP is ranked No. 9 for overall prestige in the 2027 Vault Law Rankings, derived from a survey of more than 20,000 associates. The firm leads in Project Finance and sits in the top 10 in Clean Tech/Renewable Energy No. 2, Banking & Financial Services No. 5, Bankruptcy/Restructuring No. 6 and Energy, Oil & Gas No. 6. Other top-10 placements include General Corporate Practice No. 9, Securities/Capital Markets No. 9, Mergers & Acquisitions No. 10 and Private Equity No. 10, with additional recognition in Securities Litigation No. 19, General Commercial Litigation No. 21, Business Outlook No. 30 and Formal Training No. 29.

+ COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK OF MILBANK LLP TO JOIN STANFORD LAW SCHOOL'S 2026 SUPREME COURT AT MIDTERM CONFERENCE

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak will participate in the Stanford Law School's annual Supreme Court at Midterm conference, set for May 1-2, 2026. The event gathers top Supreme Court practitioners to evaluate the judicial landscape and discuss how recent Supreme Court activity reflects broader legal trends. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Supreme Court and has worked on major Milbank matters, including VOS Selections v. Trump and Wolford v. Lopez. Her participation underscores Milbank's ongoing involvement in high-profile Supreme Court advocacy.

+ MILBANK'S LAUREN DOYLE JOINS PANEL ON CHAPTER 11 PLAN CONFIRMATION AT ABI NEW YORK CITY EVENT

Milbank partner Lauren Doyle will participate as a panelist at the American Bankruptcy Institute's New York City Bankruptcy Conference on Thursday, May 21, 2026, at the Marriott Marquis in New York. She will speak on the panel titled \"Confirmation Issues/Release\", examining developments shaping chapter 11 plan confirmation, including evolving standards for consensual releases, gatekeeper provisions, and lessons from the In re ConvergeOne Holdings Corp. decision. The discussion will also address how market uncertainty is affecting business projections and analyze plan implications arising from prepetition liability management exercises (LMEs). The ABI New York City Bankruptcy Conference is a premier gathering of insolvency and restructuring professionals, bringing together leading practitioners, judges, financial advisors and academics to discuss the most significant developments shaping the restructuring landscape.

+ COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK: SCOTUS IMPACT ON ERISA AND HEALTHCARE AT DRI CONFERENCE

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak spoke at the Defense Research Institute's (DRI) 2026 Life, Health, Disability and ERISA Seminar, held April 29 - May 1 in Atlanta. Her briefing focused on recent Supreme Court decisions relevant to ERISA and healthcare law. As a seasoned Supreme Court advocate, Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Court and briefed many more, and the event highlighted the importance of SCOTUS rulings for health and disability coverage. The DRI conference is a key forum for professionals in the health, insurance and ERISA sectors.

+ MILBANK ANTITRUST PARTNERS PUBLISH CPI PIECE ON DOJ SIGNALING GREATER SCRUTINY OF ENERGY AND DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE MARKETS

Milbank LLP antitrust partners Di Vincenzo and Schaeffer, with Ceccio, authored a CPI article. It notes DOJ signalsof rising interest in electricity generation and data-center deals overall.

+ ECOPETROL SECURES $1.25 BILLION SENIOR UNSECURED TERM LOAN; MILBANK REPRESENTS LENDERS

Milbank LLP advised the lenders on Ecopetrol S.A.'s $1.25 billion senior unsecured term loan facility. The financing will refinance existing indebtedness. It includes the credit facility used for Ecopetrol's acquisition of Interconexion Electrica S.A. E.S.P.

+ MILBANK CELEBRATES 53 LAWYERS ON ENGLAND & WALES 2026 PRO BONO RECOGNITION

Milbank reports that 53 of its lawyers are named on the 2026 Pro Bono Recognition List for England & Wales. The list honors barristers and solicitors who donated 25 hours of pro bono work in the prior calendar year. This acknowledgment underscores Milbank's continued dedication to public service and access to justice.

+ STM SECURES $630 MILLION GREEN RULE 144A PROJECT BONDS; MILBANK ACTS AS COUNSEL

Milbank LLP advised STM in connection with a $630 million issuance of 6.385% green Rule 144A senior secured project bonds due 2055. STM is a leading Chilean power transmission company and a subsidiary of Grupo Saesa, a joint venture between Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan ("OTTP") and Alberta Investment Management Corporation ("AIMCo"). The bond proceeds, together with funds raised in a Chilean market issue, were used to repay a $1.1 billion syndicated credit facility that financed Grupo Saesa's acquisition of Enel Transmision Chile, the principal power transmission company in Chile's metropolitan region.

+ MILBANK'S TRUSTS, ESTATES AND EXEMPT ORGANIZATIONS GROUP SECURES BAND 1 STATUS IN 2026 LEGAL 500 US PRIVATE CLIENT GUIDE

Milbank LLP's Trusts, Estates and Exempt Organizations Group has been ranked Band 1 for Private Wealth in New York in the 2026 Legal 500 US Private Client Guide. The guide also highlights leadership within the team, noting Austin Bramwell as a "Leading Partner" and Jessica Soojian as a "Recommended." The acknowledgement reinforces Milbank's standing in the private wealth arena.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JH1-B483-RVD8-2147-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:53ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREAN ASSET MANAGER'S NEW SPACE TECH ETF REACHES 100 BILLION WON AUM, HIGHLIGHTING NEWSPACE TILT

The fund has posted a 7.63% return, the best among Korea’s nine space and aerospace ETFs. Individual investors have shown robust demand, net purchasing 52.6 billion won over the six-session window after launch. Portfolio manager Kim Hyuntae emphasized agility in active management, noting that the team can quickly adapt to market developments and incorporate new listings like SpaceX as opportunities arise.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION UNVEILS 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) said it will host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, launching on 20th April 2026, to deepen the capabilities of finance professionals across public and private sectors.Over four days the program will deliver eight lectures, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for 2026.Participants will engage in discussions of alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, and will review post-investment portfolio management from both investment-management and operational perspectives.The curriculum will address key market issues, such as the evolving U.S. interest-rate environment and the implications of the institutionalization of stablecoins. Interest in the academy is high, with applications from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, KIC's CEO, stressed that the organization will continue to develop domestic finance by sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network, and that the academy will nurture global investment professionals through initiatives like this program. Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals.

+ KIC NAMES JAEHYUN JOO AS CRO TO BOLSTER RISK MANAGEMENT AND INVESTMENT OVERSIGHT

Korea Investment Corporation has appointed Jaehyun Joo as Chief Risk Officer and Executive Vice President of the Investment Management Division. The move was disclosed on April 17, 2026, after a thorough review by the Executive Committee, and Joo will begin a three-year term immediately.Joo’s career spans more than two decades in financial risk and policy. He started at Korea Ratings KR in 1999 before moving to the Bank of Korea in December 2005, where he held multiple leadership roles in the investment domain.During his time at these institutions, he led the Investment Strategy Team, the Investment Planning Team, and most recently the Reserve Management Group, gaining broad exposure to portfolio construction and risk analytics.At KIC, Joo will aim to leverage his international finance experience to strengthen the corporation’s investment-management framework, reinforcing a foundation for long-term, stable asset management amid shifting global markets. His appointment is expected to bolster KIC’s risk management capabilities and support the firm’s broader strategic objectives.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CIC SELLS $1 BILLION PORTFOLIO TO GOLDMAN AND ARDIAN AT MIXED DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs Asset Management and Ardian have agreed to acquire a $1 billion CIC portfolio at discounts, marking a significant entry in the secondary market. Ardian secured a double-digit markdown on its portion, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, according to people familiar with the matter, with the deal dated 15th April 2026.The seller, China Investment Corporation, is moving to rebalance its holdings and reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. As of 2024, about 48.5% of CIC's assets were in alternatives, and around 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally. CIC had previously explored offloading stakes in funds managed by Carlyle, KKR and TPG, paused the process, and then reopened discussions that led to the sale to Goldman and Ardian.For Goldman and Ardian, the acquisition expands their scale in the highly active secondary market. Ardian has recently raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion. The deal also mirrors a broader trend among large investors—Harvard and several Asian asset managers among them—turning to secondaries to obtain liquidity and rebalance portfolios amid concerns about prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk.

+ BLUEFIVE TO LEAD GP ROLE IN CICC-HBIS FUND OF FUNDS TARGETING CROSS-BORDER INDUSTRIAL UPGRADES

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The appointment was formalized at a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds aims to raise RMB32 billion over 15 years and will invest through a mix of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS's industrial priorities, spanning traditional sectors and emerging areas such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.The vehicle will focus on cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading, with its governance built around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—intended to connect local governments, international capital, and industrial developers. BlueFive will partner with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to help ensure long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, noted the firm’s optimism about China’s long-term growth potential and highlighted opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion, especially in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group is among the world’s largest steel materials producers and has been a Fortune Global 500 member for 17 consecutive years. CICC, based in Beijing, offers a full suite of financial services including investment banking, asset management, and private equity, and the partnership with BlueFive aims to boost regional investment opportunities and industrial growth.

+ CANADA SEES $60 BILLION IN FDI FROM CHINA AND HONG KONG; US REMAINS MAJOR SOURCE

Canada's foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong exceeded $60 billion through 2023, making these regions the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta accounted for roughly half of the total, with Statistics Canada recording $24.9 billion from China and $32.8 billion from Hong Kong, behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.As of December 30, 2025, The Globe and Mail reported that Alberta’s engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors remained strong despite strained federal relations with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse said federal-provincial discussions had intensified, describing the moment as 'harvest time' for those who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta notes that the investments focus on Alberta’s traditional oil and gas sector, carbon capture projects, green-energy initiatives and tourism. China remains a major customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline, highlighting ongoing economic interdependence even as Ottawa and Beijing sought to reset ties after past tensions from the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians between 2018 and 2021. Diplomatic engagement has continued, including a July 2024 visit by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly to Beijing, and a visible thaw under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met Xi Jinping in October 2025 and is being considered for a 2026 visit. Christiaanse added that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials for engaging with China, arguing that disengaging from China 'simply does not exist.' Canada’s broader FDI profile shows a heavy reliance on the United States (about 45.7% in 2023) with Asia-Oceania accounting for 11.8% (led by Japan, Hong Kong and China).

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREAN ASSET MANAGER'S NEW SPACE TECH ETF REACHES 100 BILLION WON AUM, HIGHLIGHTING NEWSPACE TILT

The fund has posted a 7.63% return, the best among Korea’s nine space and aerospace ETFs. Individual investors have shown robust demand, net purchasing 52.6 billion won over the six-session window after launch. Portfolio manager Kim Hyuntae emphasized agility in active management, noting that the team can quickly adapt to market developments and incorporate new listings like SpaceX as opportunities arise.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION UNVEILS 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) said it will host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, launching on 20th April 2026, to deepen the capabilities of finance professionals across public and private sectors.Over four days the program will deliver eight lectures, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for 2026.Participants will engage in discussions of alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, and will review post-investment portfolio management from both investment-management and operational perspectives.The curriculum will address key market issues, such as the evolving U.S. interest-rate environment and the implications of the institutionalization of stablecoins. Interest in the academy is high, with applications from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, KIC's CEO, stressed that the organization will continue to develop domestic finance by sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network, and that the academy will nurture global investment professionals through initiatives like this program. Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals.

+ KIC NAMES JAEHYUN JOO AS CRO TO BOLSTER RISK MANAGEMENT AND INVESTMENT OVERSIGHT

Korea Investment Corporation has appointed Jaehyun Joo as Chief Risk Officer and Executive Vice President of the Investment Management Division. The move was disclosed on April 17, 2026, after a thorough review by the Executive Committee, and Joo will begin a three-year term immediately.Joo’s career spans more than two decades in financial risk and policy. He started at Korea Ratings KR in 1999 before moving to the Bank of Korea in December 2005, where he held multiple leadership roles in the investment domain.During his time at these institutions, he led the Investment Strategy Team, the Investment Planning Team, and most recently the Reserve Management Group, gaining broad exposure to portfolio construction and risk analytics.At KIC, Joo will aim to leverage his international finance experience to strengthen the corporation’s investment-management framework, reinforcing a foundation for long-term, stable asset management amid shifting global markets. His appointment is expected to bolster KIC’s risk management capabilities and support the firm’s broader strategic objectives.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CIC SELLS $1 BILLION PORTFOLIO TO GOLDMAN AND ARDIAN AT MIXED DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs Asset Management and Ardian have agreed to acquire a $1 billion CIC portfolio at discounts, marking a significant entry in the secondary market. Ardian secured a double-digit markdown on its portion, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, according to people familiar with the matter, with the deal dated 15th April 2026.The seller, China Investment Corporation, is moving to rebalance its holdings and reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. As of 2024, about 48.5% of CIC's assets were in alternatives, and around 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally. CIC had previously explored offloading stakes in funds managed by Carlyle, KKR and TPG, paused the process, and then reopened discussions that led to the sale to Goldman and Ardian.For Goldman and Ardian, the acquisition expands their scale in the highly active secondary market. Ardian has recently raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion. The deal also mirrors a broader trend among large investors—Harvard and several Asian asset managers among them—turning to secondaries to obtain liquidity and rebalance portfolios amid concerns about prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk.

+ BLUEFIVE TO LEAD GP ROLE IN CICC-HBIS FUND OF FUNDS TARGETING CROSS-BORDER INDUSTRIAL UPGRADES

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The appointment was formalized at a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds aims to raise RMB32 billion over 15 years and will invest through a mix of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS's industrial priorities, spanning traditional sectors and emerging areas such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.The vehicle will focus on cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading, with its governance built around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—intended to connect local governments, international capital, and industrial developers. BlueFive will partner with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to help ensure long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, noted the firm’s optimism about China’s long-term growth potential and highlighted opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion, especially in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group is among the world’s largest steel materials producers and has been a Fortune Global 500 member for 17 consecutive years. CICC, based in Beijing, offers a full suite of financial services including investment banking, asset management, and private equity, and the partnership with BlueFive aims to boost regional investment opportunities and industrial growth.

+ CANADA SEES $60 BILLION IN FDI FROM CHINA AND HONG KONG; US REMAINS MAJOR SOURCE

Canada's foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong exceeded $60 billion through 2023, making these regions the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta accounted for roughly half of the total, with Statistics Canada recording $24.9 billion from China and $32.8 billion from Hong Kong, behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.As of December 30, 2025, The Globe and Mail reported that Alberta’s engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors remained strong despite strained federal relations with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse said federal-provincial discussions had intensified, describing the moment as 'harvest time' for those who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta notes that the investments focus on Alberta’s traditional oil and gas sector, carbon capture projects, green-energy initiatives and tourism. China remains a major customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline, highlighting ongoing economic interdependence even as Ottawa and Beijing sought to reset ties after past tensions from the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians between 2018 and 2021. Diplomatic engagement has continued, including a July 2024 visit by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly to Beijing, and a visible thaw under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met Xi Jinping in October 2025 and is being considered for a 2026 visit. Christiaanse added that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials for engaging with China, arguing that disengaging from China 'simply does not exist.' Canada’s broader FDI profile shows a heavy reliance on the United States (about 45.7% in 2023) with Asia-Oceania accounting for 11.8% (led by Japan, Hong Kong and China).

The partnership is expected to bolster investment opportunities and regional growth.

+ INVEST ALBERTA NOTES RENEWED CHINESE INTEREST AS CANADA ATTRACTS $60 BILLION IN FDI

China and Hong Kong’s foreign direct investment into Canada surpassed $60 billion by the end of 2023, making them the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta attracted roughly half of the total, with China at $24.9 billion and Hong Kong at $32.8 billion, according to Statistics Canada, placing them behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.The Globe and Mail, in a December 30, 2025 report, highlighted sustained Alberta–China/Hong Kong engagement despite federal tensions with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse noted rising talk with federal authorities and described the period as 'harvest time' for investors who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta says the investments are centered on oil and gas, carbon capture, green-energy projects and tourism in Alberta, with China serving as a key customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline. The relationship has shifted since the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians from 2018 to 2021, with policy steps such as a 2024 visit to Beijing by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly and a potential 2026 visit linked to a broader diplomatic reset. While ties evolve, Canada remains far more exposed to the United States for FDI (about 45.7% in 2023) than to Asia-Oceania (11.8%), where Japan, Hong Kong and China play lead roles.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Owens Corning, a Fortune 500 company headquartered in Toledo, Ohio, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: OC), is a global leader in building and construction materials, specializing in the manufacturing and distribution of insulation, roofing, and fiberglass composites. The company's core activities focus on developing innovative products for residential, commercial, and industrial applications, emphasizing energy efficiency, durability, and sustainability. In recent years, its composites segment has emerged as the fastest-growing area, driven by rising demand in automotive, aerospace, and renewable energy sectors, such as wind turbine blades, due to the shift toward lightweight materials and eco-friendly solutions. Meanwhile, the roofing business has expanded rapidly amid increased construction and renovation trends, supported by Owens Corning's investments in advanced technologies and strategic acquisitions to enhance market presence and operational efficiency. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Building materials & fixtures company by market capitalisation.

```ARTICLE

Arrow Electronics, Inc. (NYSE:ARW) distinguishes itself as a leader in technology integration and distribution, offering a comprehensive suite of solutions, including the Arrow Electronics reference design board and Arrow reference design board Bourns Microchip Amphenol. With the Arrow evaluation board and Arrow development kit, the company supports engineers and developers globally. The Arrow demo board Microchip and Arrow Microchip reference design, featuring Arrow embedded development board Microchip, showcase the firm's commitment to innovation. Critical for various applications are the Arrow Bourns protection reference design and Arrow board with Bourns surge protection. The Arrow TVS diode reference Bourns, as well as the Arrow Amphenol connector reference board, demonstrate robust electronic solutions. Specializing in Arrow board with Amphenol connectors and Arrow connector evaluation board Amphenol, Arrow ensures connectivity. Enhancing modern needs, the Arrow IoT reference design board and Arrow power management reference design stand out, complemented by extensive resources such as the Arrow electronics design resources reference board and Arrow development board for embedded systems.
```
