3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 427 words

Thu Apr 9, 2026: Owens Corning Maintains Strong Ratings Amid Market Challenges

Fitch Ratings has affirmed Owens Corning's Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) and unsecured debt ratings at 'BBB+', alongside a Short-Term IDR and CP rating at 'F1'. This affirmation underscores Owens Corning's robust market position across its core business segments, strong brand recognition, and a disciplined approach to capital allocation. As of 10th April 2026, the ratings reflect a balanced product portfolio and a diversified geographic presence, although they also highlight challenges related to market cyclicality and volatile raw material costs.

Owens Corning's financial metrics indicate modest leverage, with an EBITDA leverage ratio of 2.2x at the end of 2025. Fitch anticipates this figure will rise to 2.6x by the end of 2026 due to expected lower margins. While this is slightly above the negative sensitivity threshold for the 'BBB+' rating, Fitch projects that leverage will stabilize at or below 2.3x by the end of 2027 as revenue growth and margin recovery take hold. The company's disciplined capital allocation strategy, aimed at supporting strategic acquisitions while managing leverage, will be crucial in navigating this period.

In a subdued demand environment, Fitch expects Owens Corning's organic revenues to decline by 3.5% to 4.5% in 2026, with pressures stemming from flat existing home sales and reduced spending on repair and remodel projects. The ongoing geopolitical tensions, particularly related to Iran, have exacerbated inflation risks, leading to higher oil prices. This scenario is likely to keep mortgage rates elevated, impacting buyer sentiment during the critical spring selling season. Fitch forecasts that single-family housing starts will experience a slight decline in the low single digits, further complicating the market landscape for Owens Corning.

Despite these challenges, Owens Corning is expected to maintain strong profitability, with EBITDA margins projected at 19.5% to 20.5% in 2026 and improving to 21% to 22% in 2027. The company's ability to adjust selling prices in response to inflationary pressures, particularly in its roofing segment, has historically supported its margins. Meanwhile, free cash flow (FCF) margins are anticipated to settle around 3% to 4% in 2026, improving to 5% to 6% in 2027, as Owens Corning balances elevated capital expenditures with shareholder returns.

Owens Corning's leading positions in roofing, insulation, and door segments provide it with significant pricing power and market stability. Fitch believes that the company's diversified product portfolio and geographical reach will cushion against downturns in specific markets. As Owens Corning continues to evaluate strategic acquisition opportunities while adhering to a disciplined capital allocation framework, its strong liquidity position—supported by $345 million in cash and $1.45 billion in borrowing availability—positions the company favorably for the future.

3.2 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 237 words

Apr 27, 2026: Owens Corning Faces Heightened Buyer Concentration as QXO-TopBuild Combination Becomes Largest U.S. Insulation Distribution-Installation Customer

QXO, Inc. announced on April 19, 2026 a definitive agreement to acquire TopBuild Corp. for approximately $17 billion. According to industry reports, the transaction values each TopBuild share at $505, representing a premium of 19.8% to TopBuild's 60-day volume-weighted average price and 23.1% to TopBuild's closing price on Friday, April 17, 2026.

Structured as 45% cash and 55% QXO stock, the deal will combine TopBuild's position as the largest insulation distributor and installer in North America with QXO's existing roofing and lumber platforms. The transaction is expected to close in Q3 2026 and will create the second-largest publicly traded building products distributor in North America with combined revenue exceeding $18 billion.

Impact on Owens Corning: OC

Following this news, Owens Corning faces meaningful supply-chain and customer-concentration considerations as a leading manufacturer of fiberglass insulation, where TopBuild is a major distribution and installation customer. The combined QXO-TopBuild entity becomes one of the largest single buyers of insulation materials in North America, increasing buyer concentration risk.

Operationally, Owens Corning may face renegotiated supply terms, volume rebate structures, and longer-term contracting arrangements as the combined entity exercises enhanced purchasing power. Strategically, the consolidation may accelerate Owens Corning's evaluation of direct-to-builder channel strategies, vertical integration opportunities, or expanded service offerings. QXO's targeted exposure to data center projects may partially offset concentration concerns through total volume growth.

ECO NEWS 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 299 words

Apr 21, 2026: QXO's $17 Billion TopBuild Acquisition Consolidates Owens Corning's Largest Customer Relationship Into a Bigger Counterparty

QXO, Inc. announced a definitive agreement on April 19, 2026 to acquire TopBuild Corp. in a cash-and-stock transaction valued at approximately $17 billion, or $505 per share, representing a 23.1% premium to TopBuild's April 17 closing price. The consideration is structured as roughly 45% cash and 55% QXO common stock, with TopBuild shareholders able to elect $505 in cash or 20.2 QXO shares per BLD share, subject to proration.

Market data indicates the deal creates the second-largest publicly traded building products distributor in North America, with combined revenue exceeding $18 billion and adjusted EBITDA above $2 billion. QXO targets approximately $300 million in run-rate cost synergies. Upon closing, which is expected in Q3 2026, the combined company will operate roughly 1,150 locations across all 50 U.S. states and seven Canadian provinces with about 28,000 employees.

According to industry reports, the transaction follows QXO's April 1, 2026 closing of Kodiak Building Partners for $2.25 billion and its 2025 acquisition of Beacon Roofing Supply.

Impact on Owens Corning

Following this news, Owens Corning faces the emergence of its largest-ever single customer in North American insulation, as the combined QXO-TopBuild entity becomes the dominant buyer of fiberglass, mineral wool, and foam insulation in the region. TopBuild has historically been Owens Corning's most important insulation distribution customer, and that concentration now intensifies under QXO ownership.

Pricing negotiations, volume rebate structures, and new-product introductions will be conducted with a larger counterparty operating at greater procurement scale. The combined QXO platform could also drive incremental pull-through volume for Owens Corning's broader portfolio, including roofing shingles.

Based on strategic indicators, Owens Corning may explore diversifying its insulation channel mix by deepening relationships with IBP, GMS, the home centers, and direct-to-builder programs.

SECTION 4 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 8, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 01 2026: Owens Corning Elevates Todd Fister to Executive Vice President Role

Owens Corning has announced the promotion of Todd Fister to Executive Vice President and Chief Financial and Operating Officer, effective immediately. This strategic move, revealed on 4th May 2026, underscores the company's commitment to operational excellence and integrated execution as it seeks to accelerate organic growth and enhance margins following its recent portfolio transformation.

Fister, who has been with Owens Corning since 2014, previously served as the Chief Financial Officer beginning in 2023 and held the position of President of the company's Insulation business from 2019 to 2023. His tenure at Owens Corning has been marked by a successful transformation of the Insulation segment, which has delivered both growth and improved margins. Before joining Owens Corning, Fister held various leadership roles in finance, corporate development, and strategy at notable companies including MeadWestvaco, Kimberly-Clark, and Procter & Gamble.

Brian Chambers, Chair and Chief Executive Officer of Owens Corning, expressed confidence in Fister's abilities, stating, 'With the sale of our glass reinforcements business, we are now positioned to operate as a more integrated company and capture the full value of our complementary product platforms. Todd is a proven and respected leader whose deep strategic and operational expertise will be critical as we unlock efficiencies and strengthen execution.'

In conjunction with this promotion, Owens Corning plans to initiate an external search for a dedicated Chief Financial Officer. This search reflects the company's aim to ensure that its financial leadership aligns with its strategic priorities, especially as it navigates through a period of operational integration and growth.

As Owens Corning continues to evolve, Fister's expanded role is expected to play a pivotal part in the company's efforts to enhance its market position. The leadership change comes at a crucial time, as Owens Corning aims to leverage its portfolio transformation to drive future success in the building products industry.

Recent News 2 of 8, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 01 2026: Owens Corning Elevates Todd Fister to Executive Vice President Role

Owens Corning has announced the promotion of Todd Fister to the position of Executive Vice President and Chief Financial and Operating Officer. This strategic move, effective immediately, comes on 4th May 2026, as the company aims to enhance its operational efficiency and drive growth across its business segments.

In his new role, Fister will be tasked with executing key enterprise initiatives that focus on generating growth and improving performance. By leveraging what Owens Corning refers to as The OC Advantages, which include the company’s strong brand, exceptional commercial capabilities, leading technology, and competitive cost structure, Fister will work to streamline the company’s integrated go-to-market strategy. This approach aims to simplify and standardize processes across the enterprise, enabling Owens Corning to operate more cohesively.

According to Owens Corning, these initiatives are designed to structurally lower costs and improve capital efficiency, allowing for the redeployment of savings towards organic growth and disciplined capital allocation. Fister, who has served as the Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer since 2023, will now operate in a dual capacity, providing both operational and financial leadership while the company conducts an external search for a new Chief Financial Officer.

Brian Chambers, CEO of Owens Corning, expressed confidence in Fister’s leadership, stating, 'Over the past few years, we have reshaped Owens Corning into a focused, branded building products leader in North America and Europe. With the sale of our glass reinforcements business, we are now positioned to operate as a more integrated company and capture the full value of our complementary product platforms.' Chambers emphasized that Fister's extensive experience and knowledge of the building products industry would be invaluable in unlocking efficiencies and accelerating organic growth.

As Owens Corning continues to evolve and focus on its core strengths, the promotion of Todd Fister to this critical role signals a commitment to enhancing operational effectiveness and delivering greater value to customers and shareholders alike.

May 01 2026: Owens Corning Elevates Todd Fister to CFO and COO Role

Owens Corning has promoted Todd Fister to executive vice president and chief financial and operating officer, expanding his responsibilities as the building products manufacturer looks to accelerate organic growth and improve enterprise performance. This appointment is effective immediately and comes as Owens Corning continues reshaping its portfolio around branded building products across North America and Europe.

May 01 2026: Owens Corning Completes $645 Million Sale, Streamlining Focus on Building Products

Owens Corning has completed the sale of its glass reinforcements business to Praana Group in a $645 million transaction, advancing the company’s strategy to focus on branded building products in North America and Europe. The deal was finalized on 4th May 2026, and it marks a significant step in Owens Corning's ongoing portfolio reshaping as the company concentrates on higher-priority residential building materials, including roofing, insulation, and doors.

The divestiture, initially announced on 15th April, is expected to yield approximately $280 million in cash proceeds for Owens Corning, which will be allocated toward organic growth initiatives and shareholder returns. Additionally, the company anticipates generating an extra $50 million to $70 million in cash over the next year from excess alloy sales related to the transaction. This strategic move is aimed at redirecting resources and management focus towards categories with stronger brand leverage and pricing power.

Chief Executive Officer Brian Chambers emphasized that this sale represents a crucial step in transforming Owens Corning into a more focused and integrated enterprise. He stated, "Over the past few years, we have advanced our strategy to focus on branded residential building products in specific geographies where we can build market-leading positions and maximize long-term shareholder value." This approach reflects a broader trend among industrial companies to streamline their portfolios and monetize non-core assets.

The glass reinforcements business, which manufactures materials used to strengthen composites across various sectors, operated outside of Owens Corning’s sharpened focus on branded residential construction products. By exiting this segment, the company is poised to enhance its operational efficiency and capitalize on cross-selling opportunities within its core business areas. Owens Corning’s primary sectors—roofing, insulation, and doors—are well-positioned to benefit from ongoing housing demand and energy-efficiency upgrades.

Chambers also expressed gratitude to the employees from the divested operation, acknowledging their contributions to safety, quality, and customer performance. Under Praana Group ownership, the glass reinforcements business will continue as a standalone platform, maintaining its established market position in engineered materials. Founded in 1938 and headquartered in Toledo, Ohio, Owens Corning's recent sale not only simplifies its operational structure but also provides additional liquidity to support its strategic focus on branded products.

May 01 2026: Owens Corning Finalizes $645 Million Glass Reinforcements Sale

TOLEDO, Ohio - Owens Corning (NYSE:OC) completed the sale of its glass reinforcements business to Praana Group on Friday, according to a press release statement. The transaction carries an enterprise value of $645 million, with Owens Corning receiving approximately $280 million in upfront cash proceeds. The company plans to use the funds for organic growth initiatives and shareholder returns. Management has been aggressively buying back shares, contributing to a high shareholder yield. The $9.91 billion company currently offers a dividend yield of 2.56% and has raised its dividend for seven consecutive years. The terms remain unchanged from the April 15th 2026 announcement.

Owens Corning expects to generate an additional $50 million to $70 million in cash from excess alloy sales over the next year. "Over the past few years, we have advanced our strategy to focus on branded residential building products in specific geographies where we can build market-leading positions and maximize long-term shareholder value," said Brian Chambers, Owens Corning Chair and Chief Executive Officer. This divestiture is part of Owens Corning’s strategy to concentrate on building products in North America and Europe, where the company operates across three business segments: roofing, insulation, and doors, primarily serving residential markets.

urn:contentItem:6JGJ-X4R3-RT7Y-90B0-00000-00MILBANK LLP - INDUSTRY PULSE: KEY TRENDS AND PEER INSIGHTS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:54ZLexisNexisSTOCKMILBANK LLP - INDUSTRY PULSE: KEY TRENDS AND PEER INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MILBANKS ANTITRUST TEAM PUBLISHES ARTICLE IN CPI ON DOJS RENEWED SCRUTINY OF ENERGY AND DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE MARKETS

Milbank LLP Antitrust partners Adam Di Vincenzo and Fiona Schaeffer and associate John Ceccio authored an article published by Competition Policy International (CPI) examining the recent signaling by the Department of Justice (DOJ) of heightened interest in electricity generation and data center transactions.

Source: Company Website

+ 53 MILBANK LAWYERS FEATURED IN 2026 PRO BONO RECOGNITION LIST OF ENGLAND & WALES

Congratulations to the 53 Milbank lawyers featured in the 2026 Pro Bono Recognition List of England & Wales. The list recognizes barristers and solicitors who have given 25 hours of their time to pro bono work in the previous calendar year.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES SOCIEDAD TRANSMISORA METROPOLITANA ON $630M GREEN RULE 144A PROJECT BOND OFFERING

Milbank LLP advised Sociedad Transmisora Metropolitana S.A. ("STM") in connection with the $630 million issuance of 6.385% green senior secured project bonds due 2055. STM is a leading Chilean power transmission company and a subsidiary of Grupo Saesa (a JV between Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan ("OTTP") and Alberta Investment Management Corporation ("AIMCo")). The proceeds of the bonds were used by STM, together with the proceeds of a bond issuance in the Chilean market, to repay a $1.1 billion syndicated credit facility used to finance Grupo Saesa's acquisition of Enel Transmision Chile, the principal power transmission company in Chile's metropolitan region.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED ELLIOTT IN CONNECTION WITH ITS INVESTMENT IN STRATOLAUNCH

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised Elliott Investment Management in connection with its investment in Stratolaunch, a hypersonics technology accelerator, to support the expansion of the company's hypersonic production and flight capabilities. For more information, please see the press release.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED KKR IN THE FORMATION OF ITS $2.5 BILLION ASIA PRIVATE CREDIT FUNDRAISE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised KKR in the formation of its $2.5 billion Asia private credit fundraise focused on committing capital to privately originated performing credit investments in Asia Pacific. For more information, please see the company's press release.

The Debevoise team was led by investment management partner Lorna Bowen and included partner Sheena Paul, counsel Christina Chen Marex and Huiyu Yin and associates Charlie Altman, Ali Curry, Chris Duff, Zach Ferguson, Ruth Fruchter, Emma Hauber, Patrick Zuliang Li, Lawson Miralha, Julia Glazer Needle, James Tai, Johanna Waber and Eike Bjorn Weidner, and tax partners Stephen Jordan and Daniel Priest and associates Shanna Adler and Jeannie Le.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP: MORE THAN $21 BILLION OF CFO TRANSACTIONS CLOSED IN PAST NINE MONTHS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised its clients across multiple Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) transactions with an aggregate value exceeding $21 billion over the past nine months. In addition to these CFO transactions, the Debevoise investment management and finance teams have advised clients in many single fund rated feeder transactions over the same time period.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP: SEC STAFF EXTENDS PRINCIPLES-BASED CO-INVESTMENT RELIEF TO OPEN-END FUNDS

he Staff of the Division of Investment Management of the Securities and Exchange Commission issued a no-action letter on April 27, 2026, that expands the types of "Regulated Funds" that may rely on certain existing co-investment exemptive orders to include registered open-end funds, in addition to registered closed-end funds and business development companies, which were already covered by the orders.1 The letter also permits Regulated Fund boards to delegate certain approval responsibilities under the co-investment orders to a board committee.2

Source: Company Website

+ 3 WILLKIE PARTNERS NAMED TO D MAGAZINE'S 2026 BEST LAWYERS IN DALLAS

Three Willkie partners have been named to D Magazine's 2026 "Best Lawyers in Dallas." The annual list recognizes the top attorneys in Dallas nominated by their peers for excellence in the law.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE EMEA RESTRUCTURING PRACTICE EXCELS IN Q1 2026 LEAGUE TABLES

Willkie is recognized as a market leader in both the Octus and 9Fin restructuring league tables for the first quarter of 2026, earning multiple top-five rankings for its client work across an array of European restructuring matters.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ LATHAM ADVISES TULLOW OIL ON COMPREHENSIVE REFINANCING

Latham & Watkins has advised Tullow Oil, the independent energy group headquartered in London with operations focused on producing assets in Western Africa, on the successful completion of a comprehensive refinancing transaction which was supported by over 99% of the group's bondholders as well as Glencore, a further key creditor of the Tullow group.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM REPRESENTS NEUBERGER PRIVATE MARKETS IN INVESTMENT IN FLOW CONTROL

Flow Control Group, a leading North American network of technical flow control and industrial automation distributors and solutions providers, has announced that KKR, the company's existing investor, and Neuberger Private Markets have agreed to jointly acquire the company. As part of the transaction, funds managed by KKR will maintain majority ownership of FCG, with Neuberger holding a significant minority interest in the company.

Latham & Watkins LLP represented Neuberger Private Markets in the transaction with a team led by Stelios Saffos, Global Chair of Latham's Capital Markets and Hybrid Capital Practices, Peter Sluka, Global Co‑Chair of Latham's Hybrid Capital Practice, and partners Andrew Blumenthal and Jordan Miller, with associates Ariel Gershoig, Amrita Mohan, and Amara Banks. Advice was also provided on employee benefits and compensation matters by partner Julie Crisp and counsel Daniel Bop, with associate Erica Shin; on intellectual property matters by partner Jeffrey Tochner, with associates Amy Tosi and Karen Lee; on government contracts matters by partner Kyle Jefcoat, with associate Megan Rohn; on data privacy and cybersecurity matters by counsel Jennifer Howes, with associate Austin Anderson; on labor and employment matters by partner Sandra Benjamin, with associates Alexis Grinstead and Alexis Pinzon; on tax matters by partner Pardis Zomorodi, with associate Valerie Silva Parra; on antitrust matters by partners Patrick English, Jana Dammann de Chapto, and Hector Armengod; on environmental matters by partner Joshua Marnitz, with associate Brandon Kerns; and on compliance matters by partner Joshua Hamilton.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM ADVISES ON HERBALIFES COMPLETED US$1.45 BILLION SENIOR SECURED REFINANCING

Herbalife Ltd. (NYSE: HLF) (the Company), a premier health and wellness company, community, and platform, has announced the closing of the previously announced private offering by HLF Financing SaRL, LLC and Herbalife International, Inc., each a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company, of US$800 million aggregate principal amount of 7.750% senior secured notes due May 2033.

Concurrently with the issuance of the 2033 senior secured notes, the Company amended its 2024 senior secured credit facility (the 2024 credit facility). The amendments to the 2024 credit facility, among other things, refinanced and replaced in full the 2024 credit facility with a US$225 million senior secured term loan A and a US$425 million senior secured revolving credit facility, both maturing in April 2031.

Latham & Watkins LLP represented the initial purchasers and lenders in the transaction with a team led by partners Marc Jaffe, Nathan Ajiashvili, Erika Weinberg, and Jesse Sheff, with associates Paul Lau, Aditi Sharma, Justin Deuell, Christian DeSimone, and Danielle Stobie* with assistance from Eva Gu.

* Admitted to practice in England and Wales only.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM ADVISES EBURY IN APPROXIMATELY £550 MILLION FUNDRAISING

Latham & Watkins has advised Ebury, Santander's international payments fintech, in approximately £550 million funding rounds to accelerate its growth and global expansion.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [4 Press Releases]

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: LIFE SCIENCES ANTITRUST: RECENT EUROPEAN COMMISSION ENFORCEMENT AND TRENDS

The European Commission appears to be advancing a more expansive antitrust agenda in life sciences - one increasingly focused on innovation, exclusionary conduct and strategic interference with future competition. Counsel Michael Frese and European counsel Georgios Zacharodimos discuss how recent cases highlight the EC's efforts to prevent innovation suppression, unfair practices and anticompetitive conduct across the pharmaceutical sector, where merger reviews, abuse investigations and cartel enforcement are reshaping industry dynamics.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: BANK OF ENGLAND REVISES ITS PROPOSED REGIME FOR REGULATING SYSTEMICSTABLECOIN

Bank of England proposals for regulating sterling-denominated stablecoins have shifted since 2023, but remain significantly more onerous than the FCA's proposals for non-systemic stablecoins in many respects. Partners Simon Toms and Sebastian Barling and associate Joseph Kamyar discuss the bank's latest proposals - including criteria for systemic importance, asset composition, capital and reserve standards, holding limits, legal claims and safeguarding measures - as well as the next steps for consultation and implementation.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NINTH CIRCUIT ENJOINS CALIFORNIA CORPORATE CLIMATE RISK DISCLOSURE LAW, BUT THE WAITING GAME CONTINUES

On November 18, 2025, the Ninth Circuit stayed the enforcement of a California law requiring certain companies to publish a climate risk report. Whether companies will need to disclose their greenhouse gas emissions in 2026 remains an open question. Partners Raquel Fox and Virginia Milstead, counsel Caroline Kim and Liz Malone and associate Sydney Smith discuss the legal challenges, ongoing regulatory uncertainties and implications for companies' compliance planning.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NEW YEAR, NEW POLITICAL LAW TRENDS

Spending on federal lobbying reached an all-time high of approximately $4.5 billion in 2024 and continues to increase. Federal political action committees (PACs) raised $25.3 billion in the 2020 election cycle and $25.4 billion in the 2024 cycle, both more than doubling any previous election cycle, according to Federal Election Commission data. This upward trend continues in the 2026 cycle.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 Press Releases]

+ SIMPSON THACHER REPRESENTS INITIAL PURCHASERS IN $350 MILLION SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING BY TXNM

Simpson Thacher recently represented the initial purchasers, led by MUFG, Citigroup and RBC Capital Markets, in connection with an offering of $350 million in aggregate principal amount of 7.000% Fixed-to-Fixed Reset Rate Junior Subordinated Notes due 2056 by TXNM Energy, Inc. ("TXNM"). TXNM intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay short-term debt and for general corporate purposes.

TXNM is an energy holding company based in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Through its regulated utilities, Public Service Company of New Mexico and Texas-New Mexico Power Company, TXNM delivers energy to more than 800,000 homes and businesses across New Mexico and Texas.

The Simpson Thacher team for the transaction included Mark Brod, Barry Lau and Emily Anne Owen (Capital Markets); Jon Cantor and Jemma Li (Tax); and Jennie Getsin (FINRA and Blue Sky).

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT GEOFF BAILHACHE AND JAMES HOWE QUOTED IN LAW.COM ON M&A OUTLOOK IN EUROPE

Geoff Bailhache and James R. Howe, co-heads of the Firm's European M&A practice, were quoted in Law.com on what to expect from dealmaking activity in 2026. James explained how "European deal activity strengthened notably through the second half of 2025, with large cap and mid-market transactions rebounding as assets that had been held back earlier in the year finally came to market." Geoff also highlighted "despite ongoing macroeconomic challenges, an improving IPO market in both the U.S. and Europe has provided an important tailwind, reinforcing confidence and helping to stimulate M&A activity."

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT MARK SKERRY REFERENCED IN MLEX ON CFIUS PRIORITIES AND OUTLOOK FOR 2026

Partner Mark Skerry was referenced in an MLex article examining the priorities and policy direction of the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) last year and the trends to look for in 2026. Mark provided insights on emerging regulatory measures regarding foreign investment, noting that while CFIUS will continue to address national security threats, other federal and state-level measures shouldn't be overlooked. These measures, Mark observed, will have a significant impact on investment into and out of the United States in the coming year.

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MILBANKS ANTITRUST TEAM PUBLISHES ARTICLE IN CPI ON DOJS RENEWED SCRUTINY OF ENERGY AND DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE MARKETS

Milbank LLP Antitrust partners Adam Di Vincenzo and Fiona Schaeffer and associate John Ceccio authored an article published by Competition Policy International (CPI) examining the recent signaling by the Department of Justice (DOJ) of heightened interest in electricity generation and data center transactions.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES SOCIEDAD TRANSMISORA METROPOLITANA ON $630M GREEN RULE 144A PROJECT BOND OFFERING

Milbank LLP advised Sociedad Transmisora Metropolitana S.A. ("STM") in connection with the $630 million issuance of 6.385% green senior secured project bonds due 2055. STM is a leading Chilean power transmission company and a subsidiary of Grupo Saesa (a JV between Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan ("OTTP") and Alberta Investment Management Corporation ("AIMCo")). The proceeds of the bonds were used by STM, together with the proceeds of a bond issuance in the Chilean market, to repay a $1.1 billion syndicated credit facility used to finance Grupo Saesa's acquisition of Enel Transmision Chile, the principal power transmission company in Chile's metropolitan region.

Source: Company Website

+ 53 MILBANK LAWYERS FEATURED IN 2026 PRO BONO RECOGNITION LIST OF ENGLAND & WALES

Congratulations to the 53 Milbank lawyers featured in the 2026 Pro Bono Recognition List of England & Wales. The list recognizes barristers and solicitors who have given 25 hours of their time to pro bono work in the previous calendar year.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED KKR IN THE FORMATION OF ITS $2.5 BILLION ASIA PRIVATE CREDIT FUNDRAISE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised KKR in the formation of its $2.5 billion Asia private credit fundraise focused on committing capital to privately originated performing credit investments in Asia Pacific. For more information, please see the company's press release.

The Debevoise team was led by investment management partner Lorna Bowen and included partner Sheena Paul, counsel Christina Chen Marex and Huiyu Yin and associates Charlie Altman, Ali Curry, Chris Duff, Zach Ferguson, Ruth Fruchter, Emma Hauber, Patrick Zuliang Li, Lawson Miralha, Julia Glazer Needle, James Tai, Johanna Waber and Eike Bjorn Weidner, and tax partners Stephen Jordan and Daniel Priest and associates Shanna Adler and Jeannie Le.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED ELLIOTT IN CONNECTION WITH ITS INVESTMENT IN STRATOLAUNCH

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised Elliott Investment Management in connection with its investment in Stratolaunch, a hypersonics technology accelerator, to support the expansion of the company's hypersonic production and flight capabilities. For more information, please see the press release.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP: MORE THAN $21 BILLION OF CFO TRANSACTIONS CLOSED IN PAST NINE MONTHS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised its clients across multiple Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) transactions with an aggregate value exceeding $21 billion over the past nine months. In addition to these CFO transactions, the Debevoise investment management and finance teams have advised clients in many single fund rated feeder transactions over the same time period.

Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ WILLKIE ADVISES HEENEY CAPITAL ON OFFTAKE AGREEMENTS FOR VENEZUELAN BULK COMMODITIES AND GOLD PROJECTS

Willkie is representing Heeney Capital in connection with offtake arrangements and associated investment across Venezuelan bulk commodities and gold projects, in conjunction with Mercuria.

Heeney Capital, in conjunction with Mercuria, has secured offtake arrangements across Venezuelan bulk commodities and gold projects. These arrangements, supported by associated investment, are expected to unlock approximately US$2.2 billion in annual mineral export value, a tangible signal of U.S. commitment to improving economic conditions in and the development of Venezuela.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP: DOES IBM'S SETTLEMENT USHER IN A NEW ERA OF CORPORATE EXPOSURE UNDER THE FALSE CLAIMS ACT FOR DEI PROGRAMS?

Since the beginning of the second Trump administration, the United States Department of Justice (the "Department") has repeatedly expressed its intent to use the False Claims Act ("FCA") to target Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion ("DEI") practices at companies. Announced last May, the Department's Civil Rights Fraud Initiative (the "Initiative") was framed as a push to scrutinize and challenge DEI programs it determined were unlawfully discriminatory. On April 10, 2026, the Department announced a $17 million False Claims Act settlement with IBM-the first resolution under the Initiative.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE ADVISES CARIS LIFE SCIENCES ON NEW $1.2 BILLION CREDIT FACILITY

Willkie represented Caris Life Sciences (NASDAQ: CAI), a leader in molecular profiling for diagnosing and treating cancer, in connection with its $1.2 billion credit facility agented by Blue Owl Capital Corporation, providing for senior secured credit facilities consisting of $400 million in initial term loans, $300 million in Delayed Draw Term Loans and $500 million in uncommitted incremental term loans. Proceeds from the initial term loans were used to pay off Caris's existing third-party debt and for general corporate purposes.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP: ALLYSON B. SMITH RECOGNIZED AS 2026 OUTSTANDING YOUNG RESTRUCTURING LAWYER BY TURNAROUNDS & WORKOUTS

Willkie partner Allyson B. Smith has been recognized by Turnarounds & Workouts as an "Outstanding Young Restructuring Lawyer" for 2026. She was selected as one of 12 attorneys under the age of 40 for their work on the past year's notable bankruptcies and restructurings.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ LATHAM ADVISES TULLOW OIL ON COMPREHENSIVE REFINANCING

Latham & Watkins has advised Tullow Oil, the independent energy group headquartered in London with operations focused on producing assets in Western Africa, on the successful completion of a comprehensive refinancing transaction which was supported by over 99% of the group's bondholders as well as Glencore, a further key creditor of the Tullow group.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM REPRESENTS NEUBERGER PRIVATE MARKETS IN INVESTMENT IN FLOW CONTROL

Flow Control Group, a leading North American network of technical flow control and industrial automation distributors and solutions providers, has announced that KKR, the company's existing investor, and Neuberger Private Markets have agreed to jointly acquire the company. As part of the transaction, funds managed by KKR will maintain majority ownership of FCG, with Neuberger holding a significant minority interest in the company.

Latham & Watkins LLP represented Neuberger Private Markets in the transaction with a team led by Stelios Saffos, Global Chair of Latham's Capital Markets and Hybrid Capital Practices, Peter Sluka, Global Co‑Chair of Latham's Hybrid Capital Practice, and partners Andrew Blumenthal and Jordan Miller, with associates Ariel Gershoig, Amrita Mohan, and Amara Banks. Advice was also provided on employee benefits and compensation matters by partner Julie Crisp and counsel Daniel Bop, with associate Erica Shin; on intellectual property matters by partner Jeffrey Tochner, with associates Amy Tosi and Karen Lee; on government contracts matters by partner Kyle Jefcoat, with associate Megan Rohn; on data privacy and cybersecurity matters by counsel Jennifer Howes, with associate Austin Anderson; on labor and employment matters by partner Sandra Benjamin, with associates Alexis Grinstead and Alexis Pinzon; on tax matters by partner Pardis Zomorodi, with associate Valerie Silva Parra; on antitrust matters by partners Patrick English, Jana Dammann de Chapto, and Hector Armengod; on environmental matters by partner Joshua Marnitz, with associate Brandon Kerns; and on compliance matters by partner Joshua Hamilton.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM ADVISES ON HERBALIFES COMPLETED US$1.45 BILLION SENIOR SECURED REFINANCING

Herbalife Ltd. (NYSE: HLF) (the Company), a premier health and wellness company, community, and platform, has announced the closing of the previously announced private offering by HLF Financing SaRL, LLC and Herbalife International, Inc., each a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company, of US$800 million aggregate principal amount of 7.750% senior secured notes due May 2033.

Concurrently with the issuance of the 2033 senior secured notes, the Company amended its 2024 senior secured credit facility (the 2024 credit facility). The amendments to the 2024 credit facility, among other things, refinanced and replaced in full the 2024 credit facility with a US$225 million senior secured term loan A and a US$425 million senior secured revolving credit facility, both maturing in April 2031.

Latham & Watkins LLP represented the initial purchasers and lenders in the transaction with a team led by partners Marc Jaffe, Nathan Ajiashvili, Erika Weinberg, and Jesse Sheff, with associates Paul Lau, Aditi Sharma, Justin Deuell, Christian DeSimone, and Danielle Stobie* with assistance from Eva Gu.

* Admitted to practice in England and Wales only.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM ADVISES EBURY IN APPROXIMATELY £550 MILLION FUNDRAISING

Latham & Watkins has advised Ebury, Santander's international payments fintech, in approximately £550 million funding rounds to accelerate its growth and global expansion.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [4 Press Releases]

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: LIFE SCIENCES ANTITRUST: RECENT EUROPEAN COMMISSION ENFORCEMENT AND TRENDS

The European Commission appears to be advancing a more expansive antitrust agenda in life sciences - one increasingly focused on innovation, exclusionary conduct and strategic interference with future competition. Counsel Michael Frese and European counsel Georgios Zacharodimos discuss how recent cases highlight the EC's efforts to prevent innovation suppression, unfair practices and anticompetitive conduct across the pharmaceutical sector, where merger reviews, abuse investigations and cartel enforcement are reshaping industry dynamics.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: BANK OF ENGLAND REVISES ITS PROPOSED REGIME FOR REGULATING SYSTEMICSTABLECOIN

Bank of England proposals for regulating sterling-denominated stablecoins have shifted since 2023, but remain significantly more onerous than the FCA's proposals for non-systemic stablecoins in many respects. Partners Simon Toms and Sebastian Barling and associate Joseph Kamyar discuss the bank's latest proposals - including criteria for systemic importance, asset composition, capital and reserve standards, holding limits, legal claims and safeguarding measures - as well as the next steps for consultation and implementation.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NINTH CIRCUIT ENJOINS CALIFORNIA CORPORATE CLIMATE RISK DISCLOSURE LAW, BUT THE WAITING GAME CONTINUES

On November 18, 2025, the Ninth Circuit stayed the enforcement of a California law requiring certain companies to publish a climate risk report. Whether companies will need to disclose their greenhouse gas emissions in 2026 remains an open question. Partners Raquel Fox and Virginia Milstead, counsel Caroline Kim and Liz Malone and associate Sydney Smith discuss the legal challenges, ongoing regulatory uncertainties and implications for companies' compliance planning.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NEW YEAR, NEW POLITICAL LAW TRENDS

Spending on federal lobbying reached an all-time high of approximately $4.5 billion in 2024 and continues to increase. Federal political action committees (PACs) raised $25.3 billion in the 2020 election cycle and $25.4 billion in the 2024 cycle, both more than doubling any previous election cycle, according to Federal Election Commission data. This upward trend continues in the 2026 cycle.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 Press Releases]

+ SIMPSON THACHER REPRESENTS INITIAL PURCHASERS IN $350 MILLION SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING BY TXNM

Simpson Thacher recently represented the initial purchasers, led by MUFG, Citigroup and RBC Capital Markets, in connection with an offering of $350 million in aggregate principal amount of 7.000% Fixed-to-Fixed Reset Rate Junior Subordinated Notes due 2056 by TXNM Energy, Inc. ("TXNM"). TXNM intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay short-term debt and for general corporate purposes.

TXNM is an energy holding company based in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Through its regulated utilities, Public Service Company of New Mexico and Texas-New Mexico Power Company, TXNM delivers energy to more than 800,000 homes and businesses across New Mexico and Texas.

The Simpson Thacher team for the transaction included Mark Brod, Barry Lau and Emily Anne Owen (Capital Markets); Jon Cantor and Jemma Li (Tax); and Jennie Getsin (FINRA and Blue Sky).

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT MARK SKERRY REFERENCED IN MLEX ON CFIUS PRIORITIES AND OUTLOOK FOR 2026

Partner Mark Skerry was referenced in an MLex article examining the priorities and policy direction of the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) last year and the trends to look for in 2026. Mark provided insights on emerging regulatory measures regarding foreign investment, noting that while CFIUS will continue to address national security threats, other federal and state-level measures shouldn't be overlooked. These measures, Mark observed, will have a significant impact on investment into and out of the United States in the coming year.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT GEOFF BAILHACHE AND JAMES HOWE QUOTED IN LAW.COM ON M&A OUTLOOK IN EUROPE

Geoff Bailhache and James R. Howe, co-heads of the Firm's European M&A practice, were quoted in Law.com on what to expect from dealmaking activity in 2026. James explained how "European deal activity strengthened notably through the second half of 2025, with large cap and mid-market transactions rebounding as assets that had been held back earlier in the year finally came to market." Geoff also highlighted "despite ongoing macroeconomic challenges, an improving IPO market in both the U.S. and Europe has provided an important tailwind, reinforcing confidence and helping to stimulate M&A activity."

Source: Company Website
